chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.text:
## Answer

For America's Top Lawyers passoff / dummy-call handling, approved channel access is the control.

- If the rep is in the passoff channel or dummy-call channel, they can take the call because only approved people should be in those channels.
- Brand-new reps generally are not in those channels until approved, but do not hard-code "new rep cannot take it" if they already have approved channel access.
- For America's Top Lawyers, do not play the last part of Video 2 that mentions lawyers.

chunks.heading: Route If Unclear
chunks.text:
## Route If Unclear

Route to the current passoff / dummy-call owner if the rep is not in the channel, channel membership is unclear, the show is not America's Top Lawyers, or the question is about broader dummy-call/no-show/passoff procedure.

chunks.heading: What Reps Can Say
chunks.text:
## What Reps Can Say

- "If you are in the passoff or dummy-call channel for this, that channel access is the approval control."
- "If you are not in the channel or are unsure, do not take it without checking the current passoff owner."
- "For America's Top Lawyers, do not play the final part of Video 2 that mentions lawyers."

chunks.heading: What Reps Must Not Say
chunks.text:
## What Reps Must Not Say

- Do not tell a rep to take the call if they are not in the approved passoff/dummy-call channel.
- Do not say all new reps are blocked if the rep already has approved channel access.
- Do not play the lawyer-specific final portion of Video 2.
- Do not use this article as a general dummy-call/passoff SOP for other shows or no-show cases.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.text:
## Answer

Default Call 1 pricing rule:

- Do not discuss pricing, packages, payment plans, discounts, deposits, or license PDFs on Call 1.
- Keep Call 1 focused on fit, qualification, discovery, and the current Call 1 script.
- Save pricing and closing mechanics for Call 2.

Narrow disqualification exception:

- If you are sure the prospect does not have a business and is not financially qualified, you may mention price on Call 1 only to disqualify them.
- Do not use that exception to pitch, close, negotiate, pre-sell, or create urgency on Call 1.
- If you are not sure whether both conditions are true, keep price for Call 2 and route to sales leadership if needed.

chunks.heading: Escalation rule
chunks.text:
## Escalation rule

If the pricing boundary is unclear in the moment, route to sales leadership before telling the prospect. Sensitive fit/compliance questions still route to the qualification/compliance owner.

chunks.heading: Pending Or Excluded
chunks.text:
## Pending Or Excluded

Phone-only Call 1s, interrupted calls, silent/off-camera applicants, reschedules, Call 1 after-hours, and sensitive qualification edge cases still need separate approved process wording before the bot gives direct instructions.

chunks.heading: What Reps Can Say
chunks.text:
## What Reps Can Say

- "Normally, do not bring up price on Call 1. Keep price for Call 2."
- "If they clearly do not have a business and are not financially qualified, you can mention the investment only to disqualify them."
- "If you are unsure, do not quote pricing on Call 1. Route it to sales leadership."

chunks.heading: What reps MUST NOT say
chunks.text:
## What reps MUST NOT say

- Do not pitch, close, negotiate, or pre-sell pricing on Call 1.
- Do not discuss payment plans, discounts, deposits, license PDFs, refunds, or contract/payment mechanics on Call 1.
- Do not use the disqualification exception unless both conditions are clear: no business and not financially qualified.
- Do not promise approval, platform results, views, ROI, or business outcomes.
- Do not invent qualification rules for sensitive edge cases.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.text:
## Answer

After a sale, follow the "How to close a sale" cheat sheet from onboarding.

Confirmed sales-side handoff steps:

- Close the call.
- Take payment.
- Get the contract signed.
- Review the onboarding email with the client.
- Send the onboarding email.
- Book the onboarding call for the next day.
- Confirm payment in the All Payments / PayMe process once payment is actually confirmed.

If waiting on ACH, do not post PayMe / payment confirmation until ACH clears through the current tech/finance process.

The studio executive team handles detailed onboarding-call questions. Reps should avoid overpromising fulfillment details.

If a same-day or short-notice onboarding call has just been booked after a close, post in the fulfillment hotline channel to inform the production team. Include the client's name and email address, and avoid sharing extra sensitive details beyond what the fulfillment team needs.

chunks.heading: Route If Unclear
chunks.text:
## Route If Unclear

Route to the current sales/ops, finance, tech, or onboarding owner if payment has not cleared, the contract is not signed, ACH is pending, onboarding email details are missing, or the client asks detailed fulfillment questions.

chunks.heading: What Reps Can Say
chunks.text:
## What Reps Can Say

- "After payment and signature, review and send the onboarding email and book the onboarding call for the next day."
- "Only confirm PayMe / All Payments after the payment is actually confirmed."
- "If ACH is pending, wait until it clears through the current tech/finance process."
- "Detailed onboarding questions are handled by the studio executive team on the onboarding call."
- "For same-day or short-notice onboarding, post in the fulfillment hotline with the client's name and email address so production is aware."

chunks.heading: What Reps Must Not Say
chunks.text:
## What Reps Must Not Say

- Do not post payment confirmation while ACH or another payment method is still pending.
- Do not promise fulfillment details that belong to the studio executive / onboarding team.
- Do not skip contract signature or onboarding-call booking.
- Do not invent custom post-sale steps beyond the current cheat sheet.
- Do not assume production saw a same-day onboarding booking without notifying the fulfillment hotline.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.text:
## Answer

Reps should not externally share internal materials unless explicitly approved.

This includes:

- internal Slack content
- payment details
- dashboards
- source docs
- confidential notes
- audition recordings
- call recordings
- stats decks
- training videos
- internal sales materials

If a prospect asks for the video they received before the audition, do not send that pre-audition video out. If the prospect is looking for an email/video assignment they already received, they should search their email inbox; the rep can resend the pre-call email with the assignment to watch episodes and ask the prospect to check spam.

If a prospect or applicant asks for an audition recording or call recording to be deleted, vaulted, sent, or handled after they are not a fit, do not send, delete, or vault it yourself. Do not promise deletion or vaulting. Acknowledge the request and route it to the source owner, compliance owner, or current process owner so it can be handled through the approved process.

chunks.heading: Route If Unclear
chunks.text:
## Route If Unclear

If a prospect or client asks for material that looks internal, route to the source owner/compliance owner before sharing.

If a client wants a call recording, do not send it automatically. Treat call recordings as internal/private material unless external sharing is explicitly approved and permissioned.

If the request is about deleting, vaulting, or removing an audition/call recording, route it to the source owner/compliance owner or current process owner. Do not take the action yourself from the chatbot answer.

chunks.heading: What Reps Can Say
chunks.text:
## What Reps Can Say

- "I need to check whether that material is approved to share externally."
- "I can share approved public-facing materials, but not internal docs or recordings unless they are explicitly approved."
- "If this is the pre-audition video, we cannot send that one out. Have them check their inbox/spam, or resend the pre-call email with the episode-watching assignment."
- "For a recording deletion or vaulting request, do not handle it yourself. Route it to the source owner/compliance owner."

chunks.heading: What Reps Must Not Do
chunks.text:
## What Reps Must Not Do

- Do not send internal Slack screenshots or source-doc excerpts to prospects or clients.
- Do not share call recordings, private training videos, dashboards, confidential notes, payment details, or stats decks externally unless explicitly approved.
- Do not assume a file is shareable just because a rep can access it internally.
- Do not send the pre-audition video out manually.
- Do not delete, vault, or promise deletion/vaulting for audition recordings or call recordings yourself.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.text:
## Answer

Approved platform wording:

- All tiers air on the Inside Success Network app.
- The Inside Success Network app is accessible on Roku, Fire Stick, and Apple TV physical devices.
- This is not the Apple TV streaming app/channel and not the Amazon streaming app.
- VIP / Premium is submitted to one Tier-1 streaming platform: Amazon Prime Video, Apple TV streaming app, or Tubi.
- Tier-1 placement is a platform decision and is not guaranteed.
- ISTV does not work directly with Amazon or Apple. A third party helps with those submissions.
- Clients cannot pay extra to force or guarantee Apple TV streaming-app submission or placement.

Approved scam / bad-review objection boundary:

- Keep it short. Do not go down a long defense path.
- If the prospect truly believes it is a scam, it may not be for them.
- A brief clarification is allowed: many bad search results come from confusion with a separate UK company named Inside Success. Searching `InsideSuccessTV` points to the correct ISTV brand context.
- Reps can point to the correct ISTV review context and public proof that is currently approved to share, but must not over-argue or promise outcomes.

Approved value / vendor comparison boundary:

- Avoid perceived-value, vendor comparison, and cheaper-production arguments because they drift into ROI/value guarantees.
- Do not compare the cost of producing a show with another vendor as the reason the client should buy.

Webpage/social rebrand examples for the $30K license are proof/example assets. Sales Ops has shared a VIP conversion page example, but the bot does not maintain a full public list of examples. Use only the current Sales Ops-approved example/link and do not invent or imply a broader example library.

chunks.heading: Route If Asked For Proof Links Or Claims
chunks.text:
## Route If Asked For Proof Links Or Claims

If the rep needs specific review links, press, episode examples, celebrity proof, view language, or approved public proof links, route to the approved proof/source owner until the current proof pack is approved.

chunks.heading: What Reps Can Say
chunks.text:
## What Reps Can Say

- "All tiers air on the Inside Success Network app."
- "VIP/Premium includes submission to one Tier-1 platform, but placement is not guaranteed because the platform decides."
- "If they are seeing bad reviews, briefly clarify whether they are looking at the separate UK company or ISTV. Search `InsideSuccessTV` for the correct brand context."
- "If they still feel this is a scam, this may not be the right fit for them."
- "Do not get into a vendor-cost or ROI comparison. Keep it to the approved platform and fit language."
- "For $30K webpage/social rebrand examples, Sales Ops has a VIP conversion page example. Use the current approved example/link; do not imply we have a full approved list unless Sales Ops confirms it."

chunks.heading: What Reps Must Not Say
chunks.text:
## What Reps Must Not Say

- Do not guarantee Amazon, Apple TV streaming app, Tubi, or any other Tier-1 placement.
- Do not say a client can pay extra to guarantee Apple TV streaming-app submission or placement.
- Do not say ISTV works directly with Amazon or Apple.
- Do not promise ROI, revenue, leads, fundraising, PR outcomes, platform placement, views, demographics, celebrity outcomes, or guaranteed business results.
- Do not use unsupported public proof, old examples, screenshots, dashboards, or stats decks as proof.
- Do not argue at length with someone calling it a scam.
- Do not claim every bad review is fake or removed.
- Do not use vendor-production cost comparisons to justify the price.
- Do not create or share an unapproved proof/example list from memory.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.source_title: Platform Hosting And Client License Duration
chunks.text:
## Answer

The current duration wording is:

- ISTV platform hosting is 5 years.
- The client has lifetime license rights to their own episode/content.

Do not merge those two points. The client's license rights are lifetime, but ISTV platform hosting is not lifetime/permanent.

chunks.heading: Route If Asked About Usage Details
chunks.source_title: Platform Hosting And Client License Duration
chunks.text:
## Route If Asked About Usage Details

Questions about full-episode posting, clips, trailers, ads, embedding, YouTube, third-party platforms, written consent, or product-specific content rights still need legal/contracts-approved wording. Route those instead of guessing.

chunks.heading: What Reps Can Say
chunks.source_title: Platform Hosting And Client License Duration
chunks.text:
## What Reps Can Say

- "The episode is hosted on the ISTV platform for 5 years."
- "The client has lifetime license rights to their own episode/content."

chunks.heading: What Reps Must Not Say
chunks.source_title: Platform Hosting And Client License Duration
chunks.text:
## What Reps Must Not Say

- Do not say ISTV platform hosting is lifetime or permanent.
- Do not promise third-party platform availability beyond approved wording.
- Do not answer detailed usage-rights questions unless a separate approved rights article covers the exact use case.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.text:
## Answer

Mastermind / red-carpet access is included in all packages under the current meeting-confirmed rule.

There is a $200 non-refundable fee to attend Mastermind. The fee is for food and drinks.

Event dates, venue details, logistics, travel, guest rules, and any current event changes can drift. Route those to the current event/source owner.

If a cast member buys on a 4-pay plan and wants to film in August so they can attend Mastermind before the episode is fully PIF, post in the fulfillment hotline to double-check. The Slack guidance said this should be okay, but reps should still confirm through fulfillment before promising the schedule.

chunks.heading: Route If Unclear
chunks.text:
## Route If Unclear

Route any time the question is about a specific upcoming event, date, hotel, travel, guest, venue, current event availability, or exception.

chunks.heading: What Reps Can Say
chunks.text:
## What Reps Can Say

- "Mastermind / red-carpet access is included in all packages under the current rule."
- "There is a $200 non-refundable Mastermind attendance fee for food and drinks."
- "For dates, venue details, guest rules, or current logistics, confirm with the current event/source owner before promising anything."
- "For a 4-pay client trying to film in August and attend Mastermind before PIF, post in the fulfillment hotline to double-check before promising it."

chunks.heading: What Reps Must Not Say
chunks.text:
## What Reps Must Not Say

- Do not quote old event dates, old venue details, old links, or old package/event posts as current.
- Do not waive or change the $200 fee.
- Do not promise travel, guests, venue access, filming access, refundability beyond this fee rule, or current logistics from memory.
- Do not use historical Slack posts or old source links as final answer authority.
- Do not promise a filming/Mastermind schedule exception before fulfillment confirms it.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.text:
## Answer

Use `#greenlight-requests` for greenlight letter requests, urgent greenlight letter sends, same-day greenlight letter requests, greenlight-letter status/escalations, and questions about whether a letter should or should not go out.

If a Greenlight PDF or tracking sheet shows a failed social check or similar internal status, route the rep's internal process/status question to `#greenlight-requests`. Do not present the internal status explanation to the applicant as the reason unless the current owner confirms what can be shared.

Main ISTV no-show / missed-deadline / rejection / reapply rule:

- If someone no-shows Call 1, no-shows Call 2 after being greenlit, completes Call 2 but does not pay/sign by Sunday 11:59 PM ET, or is rejected/not-fit, the rep should tell them on the front end that they can reapply at some point in the future.
- The actual minimum is 3 months before they can reapply.
- If the person claims a serious genuine reason such as a car crash or death in the family and can provide proof, route it to Rich for approval. Do not approve the exception yourself.

Greenlight caps, exact daily/weekly limits, cutoff times, send windows, status definitions, and emergency-stop mechanics can drift. Route those to `#greenlight-requests` instead of quoting old Slack numbers or old sheet/process timing.

chunks.heading: Pending Or Excluded
chunks.text:
## Pending Or Excluded

This article does not approve exact greenlight caps, daily/weekly capacity, PDF send windows, tracking-sheet status definitions, or an automated/manual letter workflow. It only approves the channel route and the Rich-confirmed main ISTV 3-month reapply minimum.

chunks.heading: Route If Unclear
What Reps Can Say
chunks.text:
## What Reps Can Say

- "For greenlight letter requests or urgent letter sends, post in `#greenlight-requests`."
- "If the PDF/status shows a failed social check or unclear internal status, route that internal question to `#greenlight-requests` before telling the applicant anything beyond the approved rejection process."
- "For main ISTV, if they no-show, miss the Sunday 11:59 PM ET payment/signature deadline, or are rejected/not-fit, tell them they can reapply in the future. The minimum is 3 months."
- "If they have a genuine documented emergency, route it to Rich for approval."
- "For current caps, exact send timing, or whether a letter should go out, use `#greenlight-requests`."

chunks.heading: What reps MUST NOT say
chunks.text:
## What reps MUST NOT say

- Do not promise that an approval PDF/greenlight letter will be sent by a specific time unless the current SOP confirms it.
- Do not quote old approval caps, old send windows, old PDF timing, or old status definitions as current.
- Do not tell the applicant an internal social-check reason unless the current owner confirms what can be shared.
- Do not make deadline exceptions or same-day discount promises without Rich/current owner confirmation.
- Do not approve a proof exception yourself, even if the reason sounds genuine.
- Do not apply the main ISTV cohort/deadline/reapply rule to Daymond John / Next Level CEO.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.source_title: Main ISTV Call 2 Cohort Reschedule Rules
chunks.text:
## Answer

For main ISTV shows only, the cohort week runs Monday through Sunday at 11:59 PM Eastern.

Call 2 reschedule rule for main ISTV:

- If Call 2 is rescheduled within the same cohort week, the rep can do it without Rich approval.
- If Call 2 is pushed into the following week / next cohort, it needs Rich approval.
- Exceptions considered for a next-cohort Call 2 are limited to a medical emergency with proof or a bank block / inability to pay with bank proof.
- In the bank-block exception, the contract should still be signed even if payment cannot go through yet.
- Call 1 can be rescheduled to the following week. The strict cohort approval rule is for Call 2.

Main ISTV no-show / missed-deadline / rejection / reapply rule:

- If someone no-shows Call 1, no-shows Call 2 after being greenlit, completes Call 2 but does not pay/sign by Sunday 11:59 PM ET, or is rejected/not-fit, tell them on the front end that they can reapply at some point in the future.
- The actual minimum is 3 months before they can reapply.
- Serious genuine reasons with proof, such as a car crash or death in the family, should be routed to Rich for approval. Do not approve the exception yourself.

Daymond John / Next Level CEO:

- There is no cohort rule for Daymond John / Next Level CEO.
- There is no same-day discount for Daymond John / Next Level CEO.
- Do not apply the main ISTV cohort, deadline, or same-day discount rule to DJ/NLCEO.
- Route DJ/NLCEO reschedule, no-show, pay/sign, or deadline edge cases to the current DJ/NLCEO relevant Slack channel / sales owner.

chunks.heading: Route If Unclear
chunks.source_title: Main ISTV Call 2 Cohort Reschedule Rules
chunks.text:
## Route If Unclear

Route to Rich / the current sales owner if the question involves proof quality, next-cohort exceptions, a disputed rejection/not-fit decision, or a situation not explicitly covered above.

Route DJ/NLCEO reschedule, no-show, pay/sign, deadline, or cohort-like questions to the current DJ/NLCEO relevant Slack channel / sales owner because the main ISTV cohort structure does not apply there.

chunks.heading: What Reps Can Say
chunks.source_title: Main ISTV Call 2 Cohort Reschedule Rules
chunks.text:
## What Reps Can Say

- "For main ISTV, the cohort runs Monday through Sunday at 11:59 PM Eastern."
- "If Call 2 stays in the same cohort week, you can reschedule it without Rich approval."
- "If Call 2 moves to the next week, get Rich approval unless it is a documented medical emergency or bank block."
- "If a main ISTV prospect no-shows, misses the Sunday 11:59 PM ET deadline, or is rejected/not-fit, tell them they can reapply in the future. The minimum is 3 months."
- "For Daymond John / Next Level CEO, there is no cohort rule and no same-day discount. Do not apply the main ISTV cohort rule there."

chunks.heading: What Reps Must Not Say
chunks.source_title: Main ISTV Call 2 Cohort Reschedule Rules
chunks.text:
## What Reps Must Not Say

- Do not apply the main ISTV cohort rule to Daymond John or Next Level CEO.
- Do not push Call 2 into the next cohort without Rich approval unless the approved proof exception applies.
- Do not approve proof exceptions yourself.
- Do not create new exception categories beyond documented genuine emergencies, medical emergency proof, or bank-block proof.
- Do not quote exact greenlight PDF caps or send windows from this article.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.text:
## Answer

Use the latest approved show list below when a rep asks what shows we currently do.

The internal maintenance source for show changes is project operations, where new shows are added. Normal reps may not have access to that channel, so the bot should answer from this approved list and route only brand-new, paused, disputed, or missing-show status questions to the current sales/ops owner.

If a current show is missing from a dropdown or form, route to tech / the current sales-ops owner. Do not tell the rep to choose a placeholder or wrong show and fix it later.

America's Authors is on the approved show list, but if a prospect asks for an America's Authors episode and none have aired yet, be transparent: it is a newer show being cast and no episodes have aired yet. If the rep accidentally told the prospect to Google it or offered an episode link, follow up quickly and correct that. For future episode-watching requests, send prospects to the approved site/app/TV viewing paths rather than telling them to Google it.

For Legacy Makers info/docs, use the current Sales Ops-approved Legacy Makers materials. If the rep is on the Daymond John side, they should only sell Daymond John. If a DJ-side applicant wants an ISTV show such as Legacy Makers, the applicant needs to be passed to an ISTV-assigned rep.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.source_title: ISTV, Next Level CEO Pricing, And Same-Day Discount
chunks.text:
- Daymond John / Next Level CEO applicants are not under the main ISTV cohort rule.
- If a DJ/NLCEO applicant needs to book out a few weeks for Call 2, that can be okay for DJ/NLCEO applicants only, although reps should still prefer to move them forward as soon as possible.
- If a DJ/NLCEO applicant cannot follow a main ISTV cohort/payment timing expectation, they can continue later only within the DJ/NLCEO no-cohort boundary and without any promised hold, custom date, or custom payment exception unless the current owner confirms it.
- Do not apply main ISTV reapply/cohort-deadline pressure to DJ/NLCEO applicants.
- Do not invent custom payment plans, custom split amounts, or unlisted payment links.

License Options / reuse license document:

- Reps are advised not to send the License Options document just to compare Lite and Standard advantages.
- It is better to go over the options on the call with the applicant.
- The reuse license document can be sent if needed, but it is not advised because it often hurts the sale.

Main ISTV upgrade before filming:

- Main ISTV clients can upgrade before filming.
- After filming, it is too late to upgrade the package.
- If the client received the main ISTV $2,000 same-day discount, that discount carries forward to the upgraded main ISTV package.
- Discounted Standard total is $18,000.
- Discounted VIP/Premium total is $28,000.
- If the client bought discounted Lite at $10,000, the difference is $8,000 to Standard or $18,000 to VIP/Premium.
- Use the proper upgraded contract and payment-difference link through the current sales-tech or finance route. Do not create custom links manually.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.source_title: ISTV, Next Level CEO Pricing, And Same-Day Discount
chunks.text:
## Answer

Main ISTV program:

| Package | Price | Core deliverables |
| --- | ---: | --- |
| Lite | $12,000 | 12-15 minute episode, no pre-promo views, no Tier-1 submission, ISN app only |
| Standard | $20,000 | 16-20 minute episode, 100,000 pre-promo views, no Tier-1 submission, ISN app only |
| VIP / Premium | $30,000 | 20-25 minute episode, 150,000 pre-promo views, submitted to one Tier-1 platform, ISN app plus Tier-1 streaming app if accepted |

Main ISTV listed payment plans:

| Package | Listed plans |
| --- | --- |
| Lite | 4 x $3,000, 3 x $4,000, or 2 x $6,000 |
| Standard | 4 x $5,000 or 2 x $10,000 |
| VIP / Premium | 4 x $7,500, 3 x $10,000, or 2 x $15,000 |

Next Level CEO / Daymond John pricing and payment options:

| Package | PIF | Listed payment options |
| --- | ---: | --- |
| Lite | $10,000 | $2,500 x 4, $3,600 x 3, or $5,000 x 2 |
| Standard | $15,000 | $4,000 x 4, $5,000 x 3, or $7,500 x 2 |
| Premium VIP | $20,000 | $5,000 x 4, $7,000 x 3, or $10,000 x 2 |
| CEO Day upgrade | $5,000 | PIF only |

Same-day discount:

- The same-day discount applies only to the main ISTV program.
- It is $2,000 off the main ISTV program.
- It applies only from Call 2.
- The client must pay the initial deposit on the same calendar day as that Call 2.
- If Call 2 ends before payment but the client pays later that same calendar day, the same-day discount can still be honored.
- It does not apply to Next Level CEO / Daymond John.
- Daymond John / Next Level CEO also has no cohort rule.
- Do not carry the discount into the next day.

Daymond John / Next Level CEO timing boundary:

chunks.heading: What Reps Can Say
chunks.source_title: ISTV, Next Level CEO Pricing, And Same-Day Discount
chunks.text:
## What Reps Can Say

- Use the listed package prices and listed payment plans above.
- For Next Level CEO / Daymond John, use only the listed PIF and split-payment options above.
- For Next Level CEO / Daymond John, do not offer a same-day discount and do not use main ISTV cohort rules.
- For same-day discount, keep the rule tied to main ISTV, Call 2, and same-calendar-day initial deposit payment.
- If a discounted main ISTV Lite client upgrades before filming, carry the $2,000 same-day discount forward to the upgraded main ISTV package and charge only the proper difference.
- For Daymond John / Next Level CEO, a few-week Call 2 booking delay can be okay for DJ/NLCEO applicants only, but keep pushing to move them forward as soon as possible.
- Do not send the License Options document as the default comparison tool. Go over the options on the call; the reuse license document can be sent if needed but is not advised.

chunks.heading: What Reps Must Not Say
chunks.source_title: ISTV, Next Level CEO Pricing, And Same-Day Discount
chunks.text:
## What Reps Must Not Say

- Do not invent payment splits, custom amounts, special discounts, or exception terms.
- Do not apply the same-day discount to Next Level CEO / Daymond John.
- Do not apply main ISTV cohort rules to Next Level CEO / Daymond John.
- Do not promise the same-day discount if payment will happen after that calendar day.
- Do not carry the main ISTV same-day discount into any Next Level CEO / Daymond John package.
- Do not tell DJ/NLCEO applicants they must reapply just because they cannot follow a main ISTV cohort deadline.
- Do not use the License Options/reuse license document as the default way to sell or compare packages.
- Do not upgrade a client after filming.
- Do not create custom upgrade links or payment splits manually.
- Do not promise a second-show, crossover, VIP-to-VIP, or special discount unless a separate approved article covers that exact case.
- Do not quote old/spare pricing videos as current.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.text:
## Answer

The 20 percent rule is a commission rule. It does not let reps ignore lead ownership, Keap assignment, or DNC checks.

Before dialing a 20 percent lead:

- Check Keap first.
- If the lead is assigned to another rep, do not dial it.
- Check DNC / opt-out status before contacting.
- Keep Keap notes current.

Lead ownership:

- First booking wins if the same person is booked with two reps or shows.
- A rep has a 30-day ownership window from the time they communicate with the lead and log the communication in Keap.
- Every new contact / logged note refreshes the 30-day window.
- After 31 days without contact, another rep can speak with the lead.

- "The ownership window is 30 days from a logged communication in Keap, and each new logged contact refreshes it."
- "After 31 days with no contact, another rep can speak with the lead."
- "If the same person is booked twice, the first booking wins."

- Do not hoard leads or work more leads than can be kept updated properly.

If a client asks for contract edits, attorney review, custom language, a special addendum, entity-name changes, wrong amount/show/deliverable links, or legal interpretation, route to Rich / contracts / legal instead of handling it in the bot.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.text:
## Answer

Reps should use only current official payment links and listed payment plans.

Custom payment plans, custom splits, custom amounts, and custom payment links are not allowed. Reps should not suggest, create, or promise them. Use only the approved listed payment/installment plans from the current spreadsheet/source.

Wire/ACH, invoice, failed-payment, duplicate-charge, card-update, payment-status, refund, and billing questions are separate finance/payment operations. Those route to `#sales-finance-requests`, but routing them does not mean a rep can offer a custom payment plan.

Reps should not collect raw card details directly from clients.

Failed payment boundary:

- Finance initially follows up on failed payments by calling, texting, and emailing a few times.
- If finance cannot get through, the casting manager or sales rep may need to help chase the client.
- Reps should track payment status, but the rep may not be the first person to know a payment failed.
- If the initial payment already went through, a later failed payment should not remove the same-day discount.
- If ACH is pending, do not treat the payment as confirmed until it clears through the current tech/finance process.

chunks.heading: Route If Unclear
chunks.text:
## Route If Unclear

Use issue-type routing. Do not tag an individual person as the default; post in the relevant channel so the right owner can reply there.

- Use `#sales-finance-requests` for client billing, failed payments, duplicate charges, card-update/payment-status questions, refunds, wire/ACH, invoices, and finance/payment exceptions.
- Use `#sales-tech-requests` when the problem is technical: broken payment links, checkout/page errors, Keap/form/dropdown issues, Zoom/call/recording issues, calendar/rebooking tooling, or payment-link mechanics that are not working.
- Use `#sales-questions-requests` when the issue is a sales-policy approval question rather than a finance or tech execution issue.

For custom payment plan/custom split/custom amount questions, answer the boundary first: no custom plan; use only the approved listed plans. Use finance routing only when there is a real payment-operation issue to resolve.

chunks.text:

- "Use the current official payment link and the listed payment plan for that package."
- "No, you cannot offer a custom payment plan. Use only the approved listed plans from the current spreadsheet/source."
- "Do not create a custom split, custom amount, or custom payment link to save a deal."
- "For wire/ACH, invoice, failed-payment, duplicate-charge, card-update, payment-status, refund, or billing questions, post in `#sales-finance-requests` before giving payment instructions or promising an outcome."
- "If the issue is that a payment link, checkout page, form, dropdown, or sales system is not working, post it in `#sales-tech-requests`."
- "If a later payment fails after the initial payment was already made, do not remove the same-day discount just because of that later failure."
- "Finance follows up first on failed payments. If they cannot reach the client, the casting manager or rep may need to help chase it."

chunks.heading: What Reps Must Not Do
chunks.text:
## What Reps Must Not Do

- Do not create, suggest, or promise custom payment terms.
- Do not route a custom payment plan request as if finance may approve a new plan; the answer is to use only the approved listed plans.
- Do not create or substitute payment links.
- Do not paste raw card numbers, bank details, payment details, or sensitive client financial information into Slack, the chatbot, notes, or local files.
- Do not tell a client that a wire/ACH/invoice/custom split is allowed unless the current approved route confirms it.
- Do not post PayMe / payment confirmation while ACH or another payment is still pending.
- Do not remove a same-day discount because a later scheduled payment failed after the initial payment was already made.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.text:
## Answer

Do not turn qualification into a hard yes/no from one old Slack example. Use these approved boundaries only, then route edge cases.

America's Best Doctors:

- A doctor can qualify even if they work in a hospital and do not own the practice.
- Do not disqualify a doctor just because they are employed by a hospital.
- Nurses do not qualify as doctors for America's Best Doctors.
- Physical therapists / physiotherapists are case-by-case. Route before promising fit.

General qualification:

- Look for a business, practice, platform, or story that can benefit from the episode.
- Consider whether the business or platform is mature enough, whether the story is compelling, whether the person fits the network, and whether they seem likely to be a reasonable client.
- Some legacy/story-led clients can be exceptions, so do not create a blanket "business owner only" rule across every show.
- Minors can be considered when a parent/guardian is present and okay with the call. If parent/guardian permission or legal consent is unclear, route before proceeding.

Background and reputation:

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.text:
- Daymond John / Next Level CEO is stricter: generally reject criminal history except minor issues such as speeding or parking tickets.
- Main ISTV rejects serious red flags such as murder, pedophilia, sex-industry positioning, bank robbery, or gun crime.
- Cannabis and firearms can be acceptable when they are regulated, legal, licensed, and positioned professionally. This includes hemp and dispensary businesses only when they meet the same regulated, legal, licensed, and professional-positioning boundary.
- Strong extremist political/religious positioning, racism, sexism, or similar reputational red flags should route and should not be approved by the bot.
- Motivational speakers and personal brands can qualify when they are otherwise a fit and not in a disallowed category.

chunks.text:

- "A doctor does not have to own the practice to be considered for America's Best Doctors."
- "If they are a doctor working in a hospital, do not disqualify them just for that."
- "A nurse is not the same as a doctor for America's Best Doctors."
- "For physical therapists, unusual medical roles, background issues, or reputational red flags, route it before promising fit."
- "A minor can be okay if the parent/guardian is present and okay with it. If consent is unclear, route it."
- "A legal, regulated, licensed, professionally positioned cannabis/hemp/dispensary business can be a fit, but do not guarantee acceptance if there are licensing, legal, or reputation concerns."

- Do not use another prospect's Slack case as precedent.
- Do not make legal, medical, criminal, licensing, or reputational determinations from memory.
- Do not say America's Best Doctors requires practice ownership.
- Do not apply main ISTV flexibility to Daymond John / Next Level CEO background cases.
- Do not proceed with a minor if parent/guardian permission is unclear.

chunks.heading: Answer
chunks.text:
## Answer

If a prospect, client, or lead says STOP, asks not to be contacted, opts out, or is already on the DNC list, stop normal outreach.

Use the official opt-out / DNC process:

- Send or use the official opt-out form where appropriate.
- Manually log the name, email, and phone in the Do Not Contact Google Sheet when the request is handled manually.
- Update Keap as Do Not Contact and Unsubscribed.
- Post the note in `ft-opt-outs`.
- Cancel any pending call if the person opted out before the call.
- Do not keep texting, calling, emailing, or joining the call to wait after a clear opt-out.

If the rep cannot message the person again from that same channel, do the internal DNC steps manually instead of contacting the person again.

Before sales outreach, check the DNC Google Sheet and Keap tags. If the person is on DNC or has Do Not Contact / Unsubscribed / Opted Out tags, do not contact them.

Security/privacy handling stays conservative: do not leave sensitive details in chat, Slack, notes, or local files. Route privacy or security incidents to the current security/compliance owner.

chunks.text:
## Answer

Use issue-type routing. Do not tag one default person; post in the relevant Slack channel and the right owner will reply there.

Sales-tech issues go to `#sales-tech-requests`.

Use `#sales-tech-requests` for calls, Zoom, Zoom Phone, Keap, forms, missing dropdowns, recordings, calendar/rebooking tooling, passoff/double-booking tooling, checkout/page errors, broken payment links, and other sales-system issues.

Use `#sales-finance-requests` for finance/client billing issues such as failed payments, duplicate charges, payment status, card updates, wire/ACH, invoices, refunds, billing questions, and payment-operation exceptions that are not requests for a custom payment plan, custom split, custom amount, or custom link.

Use `#sales-questions-requests` for sales-policy or approval questions that are not mainly finance or tech execution.

Use `#greenlight-requests` for greenlight letter requests, urgent greenlight letter sends, and greenlight-letter status/escalation requests.

If the rep is asking for exact steps rather than routing, send them to the relevant channel instead of inventing the steps:

Include enough non-sensitive context for the channel to help: show/product, client name if appropriate, rep name, what happened, what link/tool failed, deadline/urgency, and screenshots only if they do not expose private payment or login details.

chunks.text:

chunks.text:
## Approval questions

1. What is the current payment method/link matrix by product, amount, package, payment plan, split card, failed payment, and upgrade?
2. Which payment links or bank/wire details are sensitive and must never be emitted by the bot?
3. What is the approved card-update/manual-charge process?
4. What exact fields should reps include when escalating a payment issue?
5. What outcomes can finance approve for duplicate charge, card update, failed scheduled payment, paid-but-not-signed, and wire/ACH cases?

chunks.heading: Candidate synthesis
chunks.source_title: Payment Links, Methods And Card Handling
chunks.text:
## Candidate synthesis

- Reps frequently ask about broken payment links, custom links, Keap/Whop/Stripe/PayPal, ACH, wire, invoices, failed card attempts, card updates, manual charges, and payment confirmation.
- Slack evidence is case-heavy and sometimes contains sensitive details. It is unsafe as direct answer material.
- The safest future article is an owner-approved payment matrix plus a strict PCI-safe card-handling rule.

- Question: How strict are the Call 2, Sunday cohort, and same-day discount deadlines?
- Candidate answer: A Call 2 generally must be locked in before Sunday 11:59 PM EST for that cohort. Weekend greenlight coverage existed at the time, and Friday Call 1 prospects should be scheduled based on what works for the client while still honoring the Sunday lock-in rule. Same-day discount guidance was strict: if the prospect responds after the rep is unavailable and payment slips to the next day, the discount should not be extended. Illness/COVID was handled as a leadership-approved exception, and one thread allowed a pre-rule-change discount promise to be honored.

### Criminal history and open cases

- Source: `#sales-questions-requests`, parent `1781883027.590809`
- Link: [internal link]
- Question/context: Rich posted guidance to stop repeated criminal-history questions.
- Candidate answer: For ISTV, people who served jail time can generally proceed depending on the crime and judgment; violent/serious crimes such as rape, murder, or assault were called out as not acceptable. Open cases or lawsuits should wait until resolved. For Daymond John, any criminal history is generally no, except minor issues such as a speeding ticket. Replies clarified that fraud/financial crimes count as criminal charges and attempted murder involvement is not acceptable.

chunks.heading: Pass-off calls, dummy calls, and recordings
chunks.text:
### Pass-off calls, dummy calls, and recordings

- Source: `#sales-questions-requests`, parents `1777647993.217899`, `1777469664.313259`, `1777463186.401489`, `1777418367.611919`, `1777414736.359209`, `1777410861.040969`, `1777295389.816139`, `1777290250.152949`
- Links:
  - [internal link]
  - [internal link]
  - [internal link]
  - [internal link]
  - [internal link]
  - [internal link]
  - [internal link]
  - [internal link]
- Question: How should pass-off calls, dummy calls, and pass-off recordings be handled?
- Candidate answer: For pass-off greenlights, get the Zoom recording from the original rep first; a pass-off recordings channel can be a fallback when automation works. Dummy calls can be claimed immediately by first comment; pass-off calls were described as claimable after a short buffer from original start time, and if the original rep joins, the pass-off rep should quietly exit. For technical outages or illness, pass-off ops/leadership may coordinate by DM or split larger batches across reps. Mandatory pre-call SMS/email status matters before handing a call to another rep.
- KB treatment: internal routing/process candidate; exact channels, wait times, and owner map need approval.

chunks.heading: Qualification and due-diligence review
chunks.text:
### Qualification and due-diligence review

- Source: `#2026-main-all-sales-reps-no-questions`, qualification, due diligence, and rejection reminders.
- Question: What should reps check before greenlighting a prospect?
- Candidate answer: Captured guidance emphasized business legitimacy, online reputation/social proof, website or operating proof, ownership/founder status, brand alignment, and caution around MLM/affiliate-only models, adult or suggestive businesses, inconsistent identities, reputational risk, and show-specific fit. Some categories such as authors, nurses/doctors, financial advisors, franchises, personal brands, cannabis, and creators had nuance.
- KB treatment: qualification candidate; needs approved rubric and examples.

chunks.heading: Qualification edge cases
chunks.text:
### Qualification edge cases

- Source: `#sales-questions-requests`, parents `1777565781.003799`, `1777565710.353789`, `1777476001.052939`, `1777469708.928389`, `1777576249.112159`, `1777917188.061759`
- Links:
  - [internal link]
  - [internal link]
  - [internal link]
  - [internal link]
  - [internal link]
  - [internal link]
- Question: Which edge-case applicants can qualify?
- Candidate answer: Financial advisors were considered good candidates. Marketplace/social-platform sellers are not automatic rejects if they have a real business, strong offer, and owned web/social presence, but owned business assets are preferred. Influencer/content income can count as a personal brand/business if substantial, but OF-led public positioning was treated as a no-go in the captured thread. Criminal-history guidance was stricter in this slice than some later ISTV discussions: greenlight could proceed only with clear caveats, and active criminal charges on record were described as disqualifying. Regulated/health, psychedelics, adult-content, and criminal-background cases all need formal policy.
- KB treatment: qualification rubric candidate with conflict flags; do not use as final answers until approved.

chunks.heading: 20 Percent Dial-Out
chunks.text:
### 20 Percent Dial-Out

- Reps should not cherry-pick, filter, or reserve more leads than they can work.
- Leads must be contacted promptly and updated by the daily deadline.
- No calls/texts should be made after the evening cutoff.
- The dedicated 20 percent SOP should be treated as the candidate source of truth.

chunks.heading: Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-04/02 Crawl
chunks.text:
- The company could not accommodate non-English languages at that time; English-speaking partners can be auditioned/cast if they are partners.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: language support is time-sensitive.

- Fitness coaching can be a legitimate business if the applicant is starting it or coaching online; otherwise reps should run the audition and ask the right questions.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- 20% calling cutoff was stated as 9 PM EST.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: calling compliance review recommended.

- Vital-info email must be sent the day before, not exactly 24 hours before, and before 9 PM EST the night before the meeting.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Reps do not give prospects cast-member contact information for reference calls.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Startups can qualify if they have means to invest plus a legitimate business/good concept/website/social proof; employee-only applicants with no plan for their own business are probably not a fit.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Nonprofits can be a good fit if legitimate and supported by social proof; reps should identify who can approve/payment and get decision-makers involved.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary; peer process

- If OnceHub does not expose a valid outbound time, using Google Calendar is acceptable.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Artists/personal brands can be good fits.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Raul Rios; Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-04/02 Crawl
chunks.text:
- 20% leads should be checked in Keap before outreach; when dialing, comment "mine" or similar so the lead is not spammed that day.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer; Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: ownership duration/follow-up rules need final SOP.

- If a 20% lead's number is incorrect, it still counts toward the rep's 20.

  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- If a 20% lead already has an appointment but it is unclear in Keap, check the client's profile appointments, knowing Keap may not always reflect scheduled meetings correctly.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- If a prospect warned before the meeting that they need to reschedule and has not picked a new time, mark rescheduled and keep attempting unless they say they do not want to move forward.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- No-shows should be recorded in Daily Stats and Keap; changing a Keap opportunity to no-show was confirmed as fine.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- For dial-out scope, reps should dial ISTV only unless Raul or Rich specifically says they are on DJ calls.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- For inappropriate prospect behavior/messages, post in tech/opt out, block or unsubscribe if possible, and escalate inappropriate messages to Rich when needed.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: privacy/compliance approval required.

- PayMe is typically not filled out until payment is captured, unless Finance explicitly says to proceed.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- For pre-audition pricing/details questions, company policy is to discuss details over the 30-minute Zoom call and deeper license terms on Call 2 if approved.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Celia Sarkisian; Madeline Cary

- PIF split across credit/debit cards is possible using a custom one-time amount link from sales tech; the rep manually sends the PIF agreement.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Celia Sarkisian; Raul Rios
  - Confidence note: payment-link process; verify current owner.

- Cancellation reason is visible to the client; Raul recommended copying the client's message for accuracy and canceling immediately.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Raul Rios
  - Confidence note: needs approved tactful wording.

- The Call 1 congratulations video belongs on Call 1 when approved; it may be played on Call 2 only if there was not enough time on Call 1.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Celia Sarkisian and peer relay attributed to Rudy
  - Confidence note: verify current script.

- 20% leads in active communication can be protected from re-entering the spreadsheet by changing lead ownership in Keap.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Raul Rios

chunks.heading: Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-11/09 Crawl
chunks.text:
- Dispensary owners can be cast as long as they are legal.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: compliance review recommended.

- Realtors can be approved if they otherwise have funds and a great story.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- A partially started Call 1 can continue later if it remains within the same casting cohort.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Reps should still send mandatory email and SMS reminders even if the system auto-sends similar prep emails.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Future payments can be paused after deposit if the applicant has paid and signed; ask in tech and tag Gohar.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: finance/payment-plan exception.

- Finance issues should tag Leticia Blanco and/or Michael Kumov; a point-of-contact document did not exist at the time.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- A paid client with a weak story is not denied on production side if they still have a legitimate business and want to use the assets to grow.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: fulfillment/approval wording needs care.

- In `istv-didnt-book-20-percent`, reps can take 20 open entries not necessarily in a row; this is 20 on the sheet and 20 in the channel per rep.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Best practice is to disqualify on the call, even for clearly unfit prospects; a rejection letter exists.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-15/11 Crawl
chunks.text:
- A real artist using sexual/aggressive language may still be cast if the content is not egregious.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: eligibility item; define disallowed content.

- The contract placement/publication minimum is now 5 years, not 2 years; contracts were being updated to remove 2-year language.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary; Angelina Miller
  - Confidence note: legal/contract item; verify current contract.

- For 20% outbound reporting, reps should report in the spreadsheet and Keap. Keap notes are added on the client profile under notes after searching by email or phone.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Do not skip the Call 1 audition/script just because the prospect called and told their story; run Call 1 as usual, then close at the end if they are ready.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- The 20% SOP still requires texting if the lead does not answer.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: peer interpretation of SOP; no final manager correction in-thread
  - Confidence note: verify current SOP.

- DJ closers trained on ISTV deliverables can sell ISTV, and reps can discuss other shows when the applicant is a better fit elsewhere.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Monday follow-up is okay for high-quality 20% outbound leads who did not answer; reps should attempt a few times before letting them go.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Applicants can be international; the team does not only work with US applicants.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-15/11 Crawl
chunks.text:
## Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-15/11 Crawl

- After Call 1 is conducted, Call 2 must be scheduled in the same week, with exceptions only for real emergencies such as car accident, death in family, or illness.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: cohort/compliance item; confirm final wording.

- A spouse or additional decision maker can be added to the Zoom invite by editing the Google Calendar event and adding their email address.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- A past canceled applicant can be reattempted if they did not send SMS/email saying not to contact them.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: opt-out/contact-compliance item.

- If a high-quality lead has an incorrect phone number, the rep can email, but should keep the email focused on correcting the phone number rather than sending the full Call 1 prep email.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Calls that already started should be rescheduled through OnceHub public/private calendar links; a new calendar event may be needed in some cases.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary; Kent Felushko

- If a prospect attempted payment before cohort close but payment completed afterward due to a bank issue, proof of the failed payment transaction before the cohort ended is required.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: payment/cohort exception; define acceptable proof.

chunks.heading: Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-15/11 Crawl
chunks.text:
- Not all payments show in Keap; reps should verify payments in the all-payments Slack channel, and for card/Whop payments can use confirmation email proof.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary; peer operational guidance
  - Confidence note: verify official payment channels.

- For double bookings in daily stats, each scheduled call counts individually. Stats should add up to the total scheduled calls in the calendar.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Refund requests are not handled by reps. Reps can say they are not saying a refund cannot happen, only that refund handling is not their department, and can forward the request to finance/senior team.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: legal/refund wording requires approval.

- No Sunday dial-out; outbound dialing is Monday through Saturday.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Company policy is that everything is done over a 30-minute Zoom call, and nobody is allowed in studio without some form of financial commitment.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Emailing the License Options PDF is not advised, even after Zoom issues.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- If a prospect no-shows Call 2 after Call 1, they are disqualified.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: cohort/compliance wording needs approval.

- Premium VIP 50% offer was described as only VIP-to-VIP on the ISTV side, for any show combo.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: pricing item; returning-later edge case unresolved.

chunks.heading: Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-15/11 Crawl
chunks.text:
- If a lead says they do not have money before Call 1, reps should still go through qualification and disqualify on the call only if the lead says something disqualifying.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- The $2K discount requires same-day payment.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen; Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: pricing item; verify current campaign.

- For overlapping Call 1 and Call 2 where pass-off is needed, Rich directed the rep to M Zubair for pass-off handling.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: verify current pass-off owner.

- Call 1 can be rescheduled if the applicant has not auditioned yet, including after a serious personal emergency.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary; Celia Sarkisian

- For same-day Call 2 after greenlight, reps should add the Call 2 submission in the thread of the daily-stats post, include the Zoom recording, and tag Adedokun for the greenlight letter.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- If wire transfer is initiated with proof, welcome/onboarding can begin, assuming installment payments are not missed.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: finance approval required.

- Shoot guests were described as allowed, ideally one to three people.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Celia Sarkisian
  - Confidence note: verify with fulfillment/studio.

- Standard-to-VIP upgrade after payment should use an upgraded contract and payment of the difference; the original payment is not canceled because licenses are non-refundable.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-15/11 Crawl
chunks.text:
- Pass-off recordings are in recordings channels; search the client's first and last name in Slack to find the recording.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Celia Sarkisian

- A prospect who already owns another TV production license still needs the company's license for the company's produced content, reuse rights, and promised deliverables.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Celia Sarkisian; Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: legal wording required.

chunks.heading: Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-16 Crawl
chunks.text:
## Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-16 Crawl

- Prospects cannot visit the studio before committing; some form of financial commitment is required to be in studio.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Use the startup Lite license video with $2,500 down when a payment plan is a better fit than paid-in-full.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: verify current video library and script.

- Do not hold spots for prospects who do not have funds. If Call 1 already happened, they need to reapply to remain compliant with cohort rules.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: reapply timing remains unresolved.

- A new/no-profit business may be too soon even when the applicant has a strong personal story; applicant fit turns on legitimate business and proven success.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- For 20% dial-out callbacks, add a Keap appointment under the rep's name or add a detailed client-profile note with contact time and reason for delay.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- For same-day bookings, send all three mandatory email/SMS messages, edited to say the meeting is today instead of tomorrow.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Early-stage businesses are not automatically DQ, but reps should check for social proof, an active website or public place to learn/buy/interact, a strong story/impact, and ability to invest.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: eligibility nuance; requires careful approved wording.

chunks.heading: Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-16/15 Crawl
chunks.text:
## Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-16/15 Crawl

- `HELP` is not the same as `STOP`; reps can still contact a person who replied `HELP`, while `STOP` or explicit `Do not contact` wording should go through the opt-out process.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: compliance-sensitive opt-out rule; requires exact approved wording.

- Lead ownership should be checked in Keap by first contact/appointment. The rep with the first contact/appointment keeps the applicant unless reps agree to pass off; show placement can be corrected on the backend when needed.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: verify against official lead ownership SOP.

- The company will not make prospect-requested redline changes to the contract.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: legal/contract item; exact wording and escalation path required.

- Reps are added to Keap after their final mock call with Raul; if they are past that point and missing access/name setup, they should post in the tech channel. Thryv is not used.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: onboarding/tool-access process candidate.

- DJ criteria are very tight; a DJ applicant with recent prison history was treated as a disqualify case by Rich.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: high-risk eligibility; do not generalize without an approved matrix.

chunks.heading: Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-16/15 Crawl
chunks.text:
- If a prospect previously replied `STOP` but now wants to be reinstated, post in tech with applicant name/email and ask whether they can be resubscribed.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: opt-in/resubscribe compliance item; requires approval.

- For 20% post-call notes, Slack guidance currently points to reporting in both the sheet and Keap as the safest compliant path.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: source materials conflict; owner-approved SOP update needed before KB use.

- While show lists are being updated, mark the show as Legacy Makers and leave a note with the new show name.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: temporary operational workaround; confirm whether still current.

chunks.heading: Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-17 Crawl
chunks.text:
## Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-17 Crawl

- A $500 initial down payment toward Lite was rejected; Rich said the team cannot do that, and if money is that tight it is not recommended to sell them.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: high-risk payment/suitability item; requires approval.

- If a prospect has not had Call 1 yet, rescheduling to the next week is acceptable for compliance.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: keep distinct from Call 2 rules.

- If Call 1 is interrupted early for a personal reason, reps should not assume lack of interest and can reschedule.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- For attorney review before payment, worst case is to send the contract PDF and set another call, but Rich preferred walking through contract issues on the call first.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: legal/contract item; requires approved wording.

- Payment and contract signing are separate processes; if payment completed but agreement is unsigned, resend the contract, and escalate to refund channel/Luke if the client disputes moving forward.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: legal/payment/refund item; requires approval.

- Standard to VIP upgrades require an upgraded contract and a tech-provided payment-difference link.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: verify current tech owner/link process.

chunks.heading: Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-18 Crawl
chunks.text:
## Additional Decision Candidates From 2026-06-18 Crawl

- Reps can suggest a different show if they think it is a better fit, but final show placement is up to the client.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: verify against current sales/fulfillment handoff.

- VIP includes a podcast, but not with Rudy.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: package deliverable; requires approval.

- ISTV episodes on Amazon Prime were described as pay-to-view: a regular Amazon account is enough to access the platform, but viewing the ISTV episode requires a small one-time fee, not a monthly subscription.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: verify against current platform listing before use.

- Additional green-light applicants should be added to the same daily stat submission/thread rather than creating scattered submissions.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Fast Pass was described as a $5K option with links in the Contract Automation List that prioritizes shooting and editing, but timeline guarantees were not confirmed.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Celia Sarkisian, with Madeline Cary routing to fulfillment/contract list
  - Confidence note: verify with fulfillment/contract sheet; do not promise a timeline.

chunks.heading: Additional Decision Candidates From Earlier 2026-06-17 Crawl
chunks.text:
## Additional Decision Candidates From Earlier 2026-06-17 Crawl

- If Call 1 was in a prior cohort, a prospect cannot close in the new cohort unless they attempted payment in the original cohort and can provide proof of failed payment for FTC compliance.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-priority compliance item; requires approval.

- Prospects will not know their season until they commit and plan their Miami filming date.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: confirm with fulfillment/show placement process.

- When handling a refund request, offering a sales role is not appropriate. If the rep cannot save the sale, the issue should be passed to the refund team, even where no refunds are offered.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: refund/escalation wording requires approval.

- Lite-with-discount clients may be able to upgrade to Standard for $8K and retain the discount.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Shari Weller; confirmed by Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-risk pricing item; requires approval.

- Platform advertising was described as absent: no ads.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: verify current platform status.

- In daily stats, `Total Closed Calls` means collected payment and signed contract.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen

- Dial-out calls linked to Rudy or `casting team` can be booked unless another rep has already emoji-claimed them.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen

chunks.heading: Applicant / Show Fit
chunks.text:
### Applicant / Show Fit

- Franchisees are likely not a fit if they do not own the underlying brand/business, unless there is approval from the original owner/franchisor; the original owner joining Call 2 was preferred if possible.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Weak English may be acceptable if the team can understand the applicant for the most part.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: current language/accommodation policy needed.

- Spanish translation support was not available at the time.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: time-sensitive.

- MLM-style product/business promotion may be acceptable only in a limited way: talk about it briefly, wear branded clothing, and possibly link in show notes, but not make the whole episode about the sponsor/product.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: advertising/sponsorship wording needs approval.

- A collaboration request with no payment was rejected.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Clients may provide product/process B-roll to interweave with the sit-down interview.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Applicant / Show Fit
chunks.text:
### Applicant / Show Fit

- Love Experts may cover ethical non-monogamy/swinging topics if the content remains clean, educational, and unbiased.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: adult/relationship-content boundary needs approved wording.

- A physical therapist with three practices was treated as qualified for America's Best Doctors in the captured thread.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen

- ISTV IMDb credit was described as non-union.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: verify with production/legal before publishing.

- If an applicant is also auditioning for Daymond John/NLCEO, the ISTV call should stay on ISTV script/offer and route Daymond/NLCEO questions to the relevant assigned rep.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Applicant / Show Fit
chunks.text:
### Applicant / Show Fit

- Lawyer/America's Top Lawyers calls should only go to selected experienced/senior reps, and Video 2 should stop before the contract section.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: show-specific routing and video instructions are time-sensitive.

- Spanish-only delivery was not supported at that time because PR/text/episode and other assets would also need Spanish support.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: current language support must be verified.

- Applicants with a stutter can be okay; separate from content boundaries, Love Experts content still needs to stay clean/not vulgar during filming.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Authors can fit the network, but if they have no website or social platform that the license could benefit, it may be too soon.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Reps are responsible for basic social/website due diligence, but social media is not the only qualification factor and there was no SOP at the time.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- If the applicant's story depends on a third-party brand they do not own, they cannot tell that as their own business story.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Raul Rios

- A majority owner may pay/sign while a minority partner is featured, if both are on Calls 1 and 2; fulfillment should be told about the show-name change.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: contract/fulfillment approval required.

chunks.heading: Applicant / Show Fit
chunks.text:
### Applicant / Show Fit

- Adult-product or sex-work-adjacent positioning is a no-go in captured guidance unless the applicant is truly evaluated under a separate non-suggestive business angle.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: formal adult-content matrix still needed.

- Lack of business success alone does not automatically disqualify someone if they have an active business, funds, and serious growth intent, but reps should not let them drag beyond the allowed follow-up limit.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- For Couples Empire, a spouse can miss Call 1 in the captured case, but should be available for follow-up.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Multi-partner/company episodes were described as max 3, ideally 2.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: contract/fulfillment confirmation required.

chunks.heading: Applicant Fit / Shows
chunks.text:
### Applicant Fit / Shows

- Personal brands can qualify only when there is real proof of business/success and social proof; money alone is not enough.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Cannabis-related businesses were treated as eligible if legally registered.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: regulated-industry compliance wording required.

- Publicly marketed OF-style content and highly suggestive kink/dominatrix marketing were treated as no-go or disqualification cases in captured threads.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: needs formal adult-content matrix.

- A public-office/political brand was treated as potentially acceptable for Women in Power in the captured case.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Show switching is okay only if the prospect confirms they are comfortable switching.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Calendar and Recording SOPs Need Current Source Maps
chunks.text:
### Calendar and Recording SOPs Need Current Source Maps

- Calendar evidence spans OnceHub, master/public/personal calendars, Google Calendar, TMP, rebooking, same-day booking, and outage fallbacks.
- Recording evidence spans Zoom, show-specific recording channels, pass-off/dummy-call recordings, phone-call recordings, and missing automated posts.
- The chatbot should not finalize answers until current source maps exist for calendars and recordings.

chunks.heading: Calendars / Call Routing
chunks.text:
### Calendars / Call Routing

- Do not edit or book clients directly into the master calendar; use public/private calendars, date-specific availability, or the client's existing invite reschedule link.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and tech support

- Once a rep has passed final mock and is marked live, assigned spreadsheet calls are their calls unless a separate show-assignment issue applies.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen

- Pass-off recordings should be requested from the original rep first; a pass-off recordings channel can be a fallback.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen, Madeline Cary, and pass-off ops

- Dummy calls can be claimed immediately by first comment; pass-off calls wait until after the original start-time buffer, and the original rep takes over if they arrive.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: verify the current buffer time and channel rules.

chunks.heading: Call / Calendar Process
chunks.text:
### Call / Calendar Process

- If a prospect says "not interested anymore," reps may try to recover by call/text; if they explicitly say cancel or cannot be recovered, cancel via the OnceHub link in the calendar booking with a professional applicant-visible reason.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- If someone leaves Call 1 during the video and does not respond to follow-up calls/texts, Sales Ops classified them as disqualified.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: reconcile with no-show/reporting taxonomy.

- Applicants who reply STOP should not automatically be canceled.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Reps can color-code confirmed applicants in Google Calendar.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- New reps need both email and Zoom phone for outreach; missing Zoom phone numbers route to Tech.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Call 1 / Call 2 Process
chunks.text:
### Call 1 / Call 2 Process

- Reps should not discuss fees/costs on Call 1; costs vary and are discussed on Call 2 after greenlight.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer and Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-risk script/compliance item; exact wording must be approved.

- Reps cannot disqualify applicants before the casting interview based only on Keap answers; they must disqualify on the call. Pass-off approval requires Rich approval.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: process/compliance item.

- If a Call 1 was already promised as a reschedule because of poor conditions, the rep should reschedule first; if the applicant remains unfit, disqualify politely later and move on.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- To end Call 1 when an applicant is not a fit, reps were pointed to a rejection-letter document and told to send the rejection email after the call.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: linked rejection doc must be extracted before KB approval.

chunks.heading: Call Training
chunks.text:
### Call Training

- Call 1 should follow the current script tightly, avoid price discussion, include the required video/interview sequence, and book Call 2 only if fit.
