  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, March and April 2026 Call 1 training updates.
  - Confidence note: current Call 1 script controls.

- Call 2 training emphasizes greenlight/story review, deliverables, license options, objection handling, payment collection, and post-sale handoff.
  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, March 2026 closing framework summary.
  - Confidence note: use current Call 2 script and transcript before publishing.

chunks.heading: Calls / Scripts / Training Review
chunks.text:
### Calls / Scripts / Training Review

- Current live Call 1 and Call 2 scripts should be the source of truth for call flow.
- Newer reps are expected to submit full call recordings for review during ramp, based on repeated announcements.
- Any call-count rules, recording disclosures, or "soft yes" wording need confirmation against the live script docs.

chunks.heading: Claims / Public Proof
chunks.text:
### Claims / Public Proof

- Do not share total network views.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Do not provide granular/Nielsen-style audience demographic data; position detailed promotional-plan questions for production.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary, quoting Rudy's guidance
  - Confidence note: exact approved wording needed because this affects marketing claims.

- Do not speak to other networks/shows' reach metrics when ISTV does not have verified data.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- For celebrity names, distinguish "cast member" from "network partner/affiliate."
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-risk public-proof wording; approve before use.

chunks.heading: Claims / Public Proof / Fulfillment
chunks.text:
### Claims / Public Proof / Fulfillment

- The 100K views were described as being on ISTV Instagram and positioned as a bonus rather than the core value.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary; peer positioning confirmed by Madeline
  - Confidence note: high-risk marketing claim; approved wording required.

- Episode delivery should be positioned as 3-6 months depending on workload, often 2-4 months, with first draft mostly within 3 months in captured owner guidance.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: conflicts with older 6-8 month answer in same thread; fulfillment approval required.

- Clients requesting Instagram tags can DM the page or be routed to the social owner; tagging everyone is not automatic.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer and Mark Kington

- Live reps can include their booking link in outreach texts after calling first.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: compliance approval required.

chunks.heading: Cohort / Deadline
chunks.text:
### Cohort / Deadline

- Call 2 must happen before the Sunday deadline for applicants from Friday Call 1s, including both outbound and inbound cases.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: reconcile with later/other Call 1 vs Call 2 exception evidence.

- Missed 11:59 PM deadline cannot be bypassed because of payment amount, bank closure, or holiday; exceptions are limited to things like medical emergency/car crash.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: high-risk legal/compliance item; exact approved language needed.

- Cohort system started April 13 and does not apply to Daymond/DJ leads.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: date-specific historical fact.

chunks.heading: Cohort / Scheduling / Reporting
chunks.text:
### Cohort / Scheduling / Reporting

- Once a prospect is greenlit, the captured deadline is Sunday 11:59 PM EST unless there is documented catastrophic illness/injury. If not yet greenlit, reps may rebook and avoid sending or verbally giving greenlight until the next cohort.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: compliance/legal-approved wording required.

- If a prospect has completed Call 2 but not closed, reps should try to book another Zoom before the Sunday deadline.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- No calls means no daily stats post; follow-ups mean yes. Missed close counts can be okay if the close is otherwise commented and routed to Pay Me.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: exact current channel checklist still needed.

- Fake-number or unreachable appointments need one-by-one approval from sales leadership before cancellation.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer

chunks.heading: Communication / Branding
chunks.text:
### Communication / Branding

- Email signatures may include ISTV branding, but Rudy said the title should be "CASTING TEAM", the real logo should be used, and the phone number should be the rep's Zoom number.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: needs approved signature/logo asset before KB approval.

- Reps should not directly ask about revenue, even if revenue was entered in the application; discussion should be framed around investment range instead.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: compliance/sales-script item; exact wording should be approved.

- 9 PM EST appears to be the cutoff for SMS messages.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: compare against other SMS timing rules.

chunks.heading: Communication / Compliance
chunks.text:
### Communication / Compliance

- STOP replies should be treated as opt-outs. Reps should not continue communicating after STOP and should route the record to the opt-out process for Keap/backend handling.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: compliance article; exact channel/form needed.

- Recording disclosure should be present for both Call 1 and Call 2.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Do not send press release/IMDb examples; screen-share only if the prospect asks.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer

chunks.heading: Communication Channels
chunks.text:
## Communication Channels

- `#2026-main-all-sales-reps-no-questions` is for Rudy updates only; reps are not supposed to post there. Sales Ops may repost useful material and tag the channel if all reps need to see it.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary, Sales Ops

chunks.heading: Compliance / Confidentiality
chunks.text:
### Compliance / Confidentiality

- Reps should not send applicants copies of Call 1 videos or call recordings externally; applicants need to be present on the call to view confidential video material.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: needs approved confidentiality script.

- Reps should not use ROI-style viewer math or capital-raise case studies; this was flagged as dangerous and sticky for FTC compliance.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen and Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: compliance/legal approval required.

- Channel renames that might affect automations should go through tech validation first.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer

chunks.heading: Compliance / Confidentiality / Rights
chunks.text:
### Compliance / Confidentiality / Rights

- Do not email recorded Call 1/Call 2 offer videos to clients; those videos are confidential.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Reps may view internal slide-deck stats, but should not share the deck with clients.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Clients can generally use clips publicly, including in ads or YouTube, but full episode use is restricted; DJ/NLCEO full episode use appears stricter.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: verify against current contracts before chatbot use.

chunks.heading: Compliance / Public Proof
chunks.text:
### Compliance / Public Proof

- Avoid ROI guarantees, guaranteed lead/view claims, unsupported demographics, and unverified platform statements.
- Platform wording and public-proof claims must come from a current compliance-approved proof pack.

chunks.heading: Compliance / Sales Claims
chunks.text:
### Compliance / Sales Claims

- Reps should not talk about ROI when prospects ask about sales funnels or return after production.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer

- Podcast/platform stats should not be given out as internal numbers; prospects can review public Spotify, Apple, and YouTube data themselves.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Call 2 licensing videos are confidential and should not be sent to prospects for replay.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Compliance / Sales Claims
chunks.text:
## Compliance / Sales Claims

- Reps cannot make ROI claims and can tell prospects ROI claims are against company policy.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Reps should not mention numbers in "million-dollar rooms/events/networks" framing.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Reps should not share case-study results or examples of how Daymond John cast used assets, because results vary and this could imply a similar outcome, creating FTC compliance risk.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: compliance item; needs exact approved wording.

- Celebrity documentary content such as Adam Sandler, Kevin Hart, and Jeff Bezos was described as authorized through outsourced, paid third-party creators.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: sales-objection item; verify approved wording before KB use.

- Reps may point to Inside Success TV's reviews page for reviews/testimonials, but platform-placement statements must avoid guarantees.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer and Madeline Cary
  - Linked source: `[internal link]
  - Confidence note: high-risk sales proof/platform-claim item; needs approved wording.

chunks.heading: Contract and Deliverable Questions Need Legal/Owner Approval
chunks.text:
### Contract and Deliverable Questions Need Legal/Owner Approval

- Repeated Slack evidence shows agreement edits, outdated links, mismatched deliverables, custom clauses, PDF requests, and amount/package mismatches.
- The chatbot should tell reps to stop and escalate through the approved contract route when contract/payment/link details do not match.

chunks.heading: Contracts / Client Materials
chunks.text:
### Contracts / Client Materials

- Contracts and license-options PDFs can be sent when needed, but it is not ideal; current links/docs must be used.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen and Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: legal-approved workflow needed.

- If wet signature is required, a PDF can be sent, printed/signed/scanned back, then attached to the sale/payment route.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer

- ISTV was identified as the responsible entity in one legal-entity thread.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: legal confirmation required before chatbot use.

- Do not send the Call 2 offer video; decision-makers should join the call instead.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- New-cast announcement graphics are in the welcome email after payment and contract.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Daily Updates / Offboarding
chunks.text:
### Daily Updates / Offboarding

- Onboarding reps were told to post daily EOD progress in the new-sales closer end-of-day report channel, including training day, current onboarding section, and what they studied or attended.
  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, February and April 2026 reminders.
  - Confidence note: current channel name must be verified.

- Failure to update for three business days may trigger offboarding, but communicated sickness, emergencies, or approved pause/freeze can be exceptions.
  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, May welcome/offboarding reminders.
  - Confidence note: exact rule needs owner-approved wording.

chunks.heading: Do Not Store Sensitive Slack Details in Curated Files
chunks.text:
### Do Not Store Sensitive Slack Details in Curated Files

- Local curated evidence should exclude client PII, emails, phone numbers, raw Slack mentions, raw Zoom or recording links, raw CRM/contact URLs, direct payment links, and bank/wire details.
- Store only sanitized patterns, process questions, and approved source links.
- The chatbot should never expose raw payment details, bank details, or client-specific support cases.

chunks.heading: Eligibility / Shows
chunks.text:
### Eligibility / Shows

- Made It In America is mainly for immigrants, but also patriotic founders and Made-in-USA brand/product stories.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer

- Non-business-owner doctors are not qualified; authors and nonprofits may be exceptions if they have a legitimate author/book business or qualifying nonprofit context. Majority rule: the applicant should own what they promote.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: requires approved exception matrix.

- A published author with a personal brand can qualify for the Authors show.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Adult/sex-work businesses are not a fit.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: use approved professional wording.

- There does not appear to be a strict minimum time in business; a newer business can still qualify if legitimate and financially able.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer

chunks.heading: Establish a Source Hierarchy for Sales-Tech Answers
chunks.text:
### Establish a Source Hierarchy for Sales-Tech Answers

- Recommended hierarchy: approved SOP/docs/sheets first, named owner-confirmed process second, Slack pattern evidence third, and unresolved Slack threads as gaps only.
- Payment, contract, finance, refund, security, and privacy topics should require owner-approved source language before ingestion.

chunks.heading: Events / Mastermind
chunks.text:
### Events / Mastermind

- Event dates, guest fees, red-carpet links, and Mastermind access are time-sensitive.
- The chatbot should rely on a current event source, not old Slack event announcements.

chunks.heading: Events / Offer Claims
chunks.text:
### Events / Offer Claims

- Mastermind/Red Carpet F&B fee appears to be $200 per person per event, including plus ones.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: current event terms must be verified.

- Oscars/major premiere ticket options are occasional extra-cost opportunities, not included deliverables.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: high-risk offer wording; needs approval.

- Prospects are not allowed pre-signing in-person studio tours; reps can share approved virtual studio/studio resources if current.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary; studio links shared by Dominika Blaszczok and Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: verify approved assets.

chunks.heading: Final Mock / Go Live
chunks.text:
### Final Mock / Go Live

- To be final-mock ready, reps appear to need onboarding/training completion, at least two team mock calls, mock recordings posted for review, and required live Zoom trainings.
  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, January and later final-mock instructions.
  - Confidence note: verify current mock-call review channel and required training count.

- The final mock booking link appears to be: [internal link]
  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, May 2026 final mock update.
  - Confidence note: verify active link before publishing.

- Reps who pass final mock should receive a clear go-live next-step doc, then complete tool/calendar setup and early live-call review.
  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, June 2026 go-live instruction.
  - Confidence note: locate the actual one-page go-live doc.

- Newly live reps, especially in their first few weeks, were expected to submit full Call 1 and Call 2 recordings for leadership review.
  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, June 2026 live-call review reminder.
  - Confidence note: recording disclosure/storage policy needs approval.

chunks.heading: Finance / Payment
chunks.text:
### Finance / Payment

- All-payments channel visibility confirms the payment came through even if Keap does not show it.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: exact current all-payments channel and access rules needed.

- For wired payments, wait until funds come through before posting the sale in Pay Me.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Wire receipts and bank-verification requests route to finance; raw banking/contact details from Slack are intentionally excluded from curated evidence.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and peers
  - Confidence note: finance-controlled source required.

chunks.heading: Finance and Payment Questions Need Routing, Not Improvisation
chunks.text:
### Finance and Payment Questions Need Routing, Not Improvisation

- Repeated Slack evidence shows reps asking for custom links, failed-payment fixes, card updates, duplicate-charge refunds, recurring-charge changes, wire confirmations, invoices, and ledger corrections.
- The chatbot should use a finance-approved intake checklist and escalation route, not give case-specific payment advice.

chunks.heading: Fulfillment / Events
chunks.text:
### Fulfillment / Events

- Clients normally travel once for filming unless coordinating around Mastermind/red-carpet events; the captured $200 item referred to a plus-one event ticket, not a filming retainer.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Mastermind access was described as one event ticket, not rolling annual access; if the client misses the upcoming event, they can be pushed to the following Mastermind.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: event terms and pricing are currentness-sensitive.

chunks.heading: Fulfillment / Onboarding
chunks.text:
### Fulfillment / Onboarding

- Use the live welcome email and current onboarding/booking links.
- Sales should not promise trailers, edits, release timelines, or marketing-plan details beyond the approved fulfillment wording.

chunks.heading: Fulfillment / Production
chunks.text:
## Fulfillment / Production

- Production turnaround is usually 4-6 months. Fast pass may be available for faster turnaround via contract automation payment links. Exact delivery depends on filming date and is a fulfillment question.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Client QR codes can point to completed episode links, but ISTV does not provide the QR code; the client must create or outsource that themselves after receiving the link.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Use of client/customer logos in an episode needs sign-off or proof of right to use.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: legal/production item; needs exact proof requirement.

chunks.heading: Fulfillment / Production
chunks.text:
### Fulfillment / Production

- Editing timing was described as 4-6 months after filming, with filming availability routed to fulfillment.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- ISTV filming was Miami-only at that time; a Hollywood studio reference was a one-off collateral issue to remove.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer

- Two people can appear in one episode if relevant participants are on the call and production/fulfillment/studio are notified through onboarding.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Greenlight / Daily Stats
chunks.text:
### Greenlight / Daily Stats

- Reps are responsible for reviewing greenlight/PDF status before the morning send window and for submitting daily stats in the required status categories.
- The greenlight tracking sheet appears to be an operating source candidate, but cutoff time and final owner need confirmation.

chunks.heading: Greenlight / Reporting
chunks.text:
### Greenlight / Reporting

- `istv-sales-closers-daily-stats` is the channel Madeline identified for greenlight/daily-stats submissions.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: verify current channel name.

- Greenlight-letter owners check daily stats; normal submissions do not require tagging, but late/changed submissions can be added as a reply to the existing daily-stats post with the needed fields and owner tag.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: owner rota must be kept current.

- Greenlight letters are typically sent around 10 AM EST, but Sunday coverage may differ.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: do not state as a guaranteed SLA.

chunks.heading: Internal Process / Routing
chunks.text:
### Internal Process / Routing

- Payment confirmation should be checked in the all-payments channel.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Remaining-balance payment links go through tech, while monthly charges are handled automatically by tech.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Rejection letters are sent on Mondays to greenlit prospects who do not commit by Sunday 11:59 PM EST.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: exact letter text must come from current approved document.

- NLCEO/Daymond calls should be passed off unless the rep is approved for that product.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Internal Process / Tools
chunks.text:
### Internal Process / Tools

- Double-booking reps were told they can submit two daily stat forms and may have a higher greenlight capacity than normal reps.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: conflicts with other weekly cap evidence; current process required.

- For Zoom Phone / normal phone calls through Zoom, the captured answer says the call is recorded.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- For STOP/opt-out, the captured guidance is to remove the prospect from the calendar and not contact them again.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: compliance-approved opt-out SOP required.

chunks.heading: Keep Slack Collection Strictly Read-Only
chunks.text:
### Keep Slack Collection Strictly Read-Only

- The Slack connector should remain read-only for this evidence workflow.
- Do not send messages, drafts, reactions, joins, leaves, invites, DMs, canvases, or reminders.
- If future automation is created, it should only read approved channels and write to local/source-controlled project files after explicit user approval.

chunks.heading: Legal / Contracts / Payment
chunks.text:
### Legal / Contracts / Payment

- Sending a contract before payment is allowed when necessary, but it is not ideal and should use the current contract/payment-link source.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary; Rudy confirmation referenced in-thread
  - Confidence note: current legal/contract workflow required before chatbot use.

- Excessive split payments are not acceptable; captured guidance says two cards is usually the practical maximum.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: finance/compliance approval required.

- Sensitive wire/ACH details must come from a current finance-controlled source; curated evidence must not reproduce bank details from Slack.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: do not create public chatbot answers with banking details unless access controls are designed.

chunks.heading: License / Content Rights
chunks.text:
### License / Content Rights

- Full episode reuse, third-party posting, website embedding, advertising use, and clip rights are contract-sensitive.
- The bot should route these questions to approved legal/content-rights language rather than generalizing from Slack.

chunks.heading: Offer / Deliverable Details
chunks.text:
### Offer / Deliverable Details

- For Love Experts, Slack guidance said approximately 15 episodes per season, give or take, and typical editing/release timing is 4-6 months after filming.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: show-specific and date-sensitive.

- The Standard contract email-blast count was flagged as 100K versus 250K; Rudy said 250K is fine and Ops said the contract should be amended.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer and Dylan Maupin
  - Confidence note: verify current contract text and license sheet.

- VIP landing page was described as a landing page for the client's business with no ads; the email audience was described as a broad network list, not necessarily the client's target audience.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-risk package wording; verify against current license.

- An episode being taken off after 2 years was explained as the guaranteed agreed time, with possible longer availability if room exists.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: conflicts with 5-year placement guidance elsewhere.

chunks.heading: Offer / Package / Pricing
chunks.text:
### Offer / Package / Pricing

- Multiple paid episodes can be sold across shows or seasons, subject to package/contract approval.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer

- A Lite paid-in-full upgrade to Standard/VIP may be split into two payments in the captured case.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: finance/pricing approval required before generalizing.

- A client finder fee is not offered, but a rep bringing a client from their own network was described as 20% eligible.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: sensitive compensation topic; owner-approved comp plan required.

chunks.heading: Offer / Package Details
chunks.text:
## Offer / Package Details

- VIP appears to include the 20K plan items plus VIP additions.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Desiree Fung; confirmed by Madeline Cary

- VIP appears to include one Mastermind Event Access, described as a marketing/networking event hosted by Rudy.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: verify current package sheet and event link.

- VIP platform coverage appears to mean Amazon Prime submission, not guaranteed; Apple TV submission may be available as a paid extra.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- All three license tiers include raw footage guaranteed.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: needs cross-check against current license summaries.

- The $2K discount can be honored any time the same day.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Desiree Fung; confirmed by Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-risk pricing item; requires approval.

- Minimum first payment to get started appears to be $2,500. Lower $1K/$1.5K first payments were rejected; $2,500 may be split across two cards if needed.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Ashley J, Annalia Pucciarella, Desiree Fung, Landria Onkka; confirmed by Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-risk payment item; requires approval.

- DJ still has a 3-day refund window; ISTV does not.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: legal/compliance item; must be approved by compliance/legal before KB approval.

chunks.heading: Offer / Package Details
chunks.text:
- The 50% second-episode offer appears to apply only from VIP ISTV to another VIP ISTV offer, not from Lite to another episode unless the client upgrades to 30K first.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-risk pricing/package item; requires approval.

- For 20% outbound leads, booking into the next week is acceptable when it is the applicant's Call 1.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: keep separate from Call 2 next-week restrictions.

- A hospitalized client may keep a $2K reduction only as a case-specific exception with medical proof.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: pricing exception; requires approval before KB use.

- Docuseries are paused; start with one show first to confirm mutual fit.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen

- One license equals one IMDb; two licenses equals two IMDb. Extra guests wanting their own IMDb need their own license. Extra guest inclusion is a fulfillment/post-sale question.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: needs fulfillment approval due thread confusion.

chunks.heading: Offers / Contracts / Materials
chunks.text:
### Offers / Contracts / Materials

- Present the license tier that fits the client; Lite can be closed first and upgraded later when appropriate.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: package/offer wording requires current pricing approval.

- License-option slide decks should not be emailed. If a prospect insists, use the PDF, preferably by screen-share first.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Contract signature before leaving the payment call is best practice.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: unsigned-contract/payment/refund implications need legal/finance confirmation.

- Custom low-price/full-package proposals and special deliverables/terms were rejected.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- License documents were described as providing worldwide usage without geographical restrictions.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: final wording must come from the current legal/license doc.

chunks.heading: Offers / Packages
chunks.text:
### Offers / Packages

- DJ-to-ISTV 50% crossover discount was described as VIP-only on both DJ and ISTV; DJ Lite does not qualify for ISTV VIP 50% off.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: high-risk pricing item; requires current approval.

- Pre-cohort old leads can be reactivated, but once they book a new call they join that week's cohort and must close that same week. The $2K discount applies only on Call 2 day until 11:59 PM.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: high-risk discount/compliance item.

- Reps were not allowed to sell docuseries at that time, though they could plant the seed.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: restricted offer; current policy needed.

- Multiple paid episodes can be sold across a mix of shows or seasons.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: verify package/contract rules.

chunks.heading: Offers / Packages / Events
chunks.text:
### Offers / Packages / Events

- Only one $2K discount should apply; veteran and same-day discounts should not be stacked.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: current pricing approval required.

- Event access was described as available to all license levels with a $200 per-person fee.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer and Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: reconcile with Mastermind refundability and current event page.

- Lite was described as including weekly marketing training.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Onboarding Source Governance
chunks.text:
### Onboarding Source Governance

- The new-hire portal appears to replace the old Red Book/company-overview learning path, but not the sales-specific training docs.
  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, May 2026 onboarding site announcement.
  - Confidence note: confirm current source hierarchy before KB approval.

- Current sales onboarding should point to the current Part 1 onboarding doc, while older first-next-steps docs should be treated as historical unless leadership confirms they remain active.
  - Sources:
    - Current Part 1 doc: [internal link]
    - Older first-next-steps doc: [internal link]

chunks.heading: Outreach Philosophy
chunks.text:
## Outreach Philosophy

- Manual texting/emailing is intentional because it comes from a real number/person and creates authenticity. Rudy said if they wanted to automate it, they would.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer

chunks.heading: Pass-Off / Dummy Calls / No-Shows
chunks.text:
### Pass-Off / Dummy Calls / No-Shows

- No-show and pass-off procedures should include waiting windows, call/text attempts, thread comments, and authorized emergency routing.
- The Dummy Call SOP should be read before finalizing articles because Slack reminders were abbreviated.

chunks.heading: Payment / Contract
chunks.text:
### Payment / Contract

- Minimum amount to hold a place before cohort close appears to be $2,500, with first deposit due by Sunday night.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-risk payment policy; requires approval.

- For bank transfers, reps should wait for company confirmation that the transfer went through before posting Pay Me.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Rudy said Pay Me can be posted once payment is received and visible in all payment, even if the contract is still pending.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: reconcile with post-close SOP that references contract completion.

- Reps may share the contract by email before signature.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: verify current legal-approved sharing process and links.

- If a payment call runs longer than about an hour, reps may set a later follow-up/payment call unless the close is realistically still moving forward.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: discretionary sales-process guidance.

chunks.heading: Payment / Fulfillment
chunks.text:
### Payment / Fulfillment

- Clients on payment plans must be paid off before filming.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: reconcile with any payment-current filming exceptions.

- ACH/bank transfer may take 1-3 business days to land; wait for confirmation/visibility before Pay Me and ask Tech/Finance/Billing to check status if needed.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Gohar Anjum

- Clients can upgrade until filming; after filming, editing starts and it is too late.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen

- Mid-payment upgrade process requires fulfillment notification, billing/tech coordination, a new upgraded contract, and enough additional payment to meet the new license minimum.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Raul Rios
  - Confidence note: finance/contract approval required.

chunks.heading: Post-Sale / Fulfillment
chunks.text:
### Post-Sale / Fulfillment

- Post-sale onboarding and scripting can be delayed in at least some cases, with production/fulfillment notified through the fulfillment route.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- A co-owner can attend a Project Beauty shoot in the captured case, with production informed through onboarding.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Full episode uploads to YouTube or third-party platform sales require written permission; clips/social assets were described as allowed.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: legal/contract approval required.

chunks.heading: Post-Sale / Fulfillment
chunks.text:
### Post-Sale / Fulfillment

- Upgrade and filming-date questions from onboarded cast members should go to the fulfillment channel.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Post-onboarding show-change requests should go to sales fulfillment so production is looped in.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Missed onboarding appointments can be rebooked into the next one, and clients can also use the link to rebook themselves.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer

chunks.heading: Pricing / Cohort
chunks.text:
### Pricing / Cohort

- The $2K discount is same-day for Call 2 and should not be extended to the next day for compliance/fairness reasons.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer and Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-risk pricing rule; exact wording needs approval.

- If Call 2 is legitimately pushed within the same cohort, the same-day discount can apply on the later Call 2 day.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: must be reconciled with strict no-extension guidance.

- If the applicant has not yet had Call 1 or been greenlit, next-week rescheduling can be okay; after greenlight, reapplication is generally no-go without catastrophic-event proof or leadership approval.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer

chunks.heading: Pricing / Payment / Contract
chunks.text:
### Pricing / Payment / Contract

- Company process was stated as payment first, then contract signing.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: reconcile with special owner-approved exceptions.

- Contract-now/payment-later exceptions should require a genuine special reason, not generic funds issues; Rudy also warned the AI report was early beta and should not be trusted for internal rules/processes.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: important FAQ-chatbot guardrail: never let AI-generated coaching reports override approved KB/source rules.

- The $2K discount can be used the next day only if the prior scheduled second call was not actually conducted or pitched.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: high-risk discount item; owner approval required.

- If a contract is signed but the deposit is not paid on the call, the discount was described as not valid; it is only for that day when they pay on the call.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Mike Wisner
  - Confidence note: reconcile with same-day and exception threads.

- Upgrade from Lite to Standard should not require a second PayMe; communicate with fulfillment, get updated contracts signed, and charge the payment method for the upgrade.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: finance/fulfillment confirmation required.

- Termination letters were described as based on the All Payments channel, not PayMe; ACH payments may need notes/communication if payment is pending but not visible yet.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer and Adedokun Adedoyin
  - Confidence note: finance workflow required before KB use.

chunks.heading: Product / Fulfillment Routing
chunks.text:
### Product / Fulfillment Routing

- ISTV and DJ/NLCEO have different deliverables and routing; untrained/unassigned reps should not sell DJ/NLCEO, and immediate cross-sell can muddy separate offers.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: current assigned rep list and product sheets needed.

- Reps should not create or send unapproved client-facing production/asset documents; fulfillment, design, and production own those resources.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Raul Rios

- Post-sale filming availability should be checked through the booking/welcome-email route, and onboarding plus scripting should normally happen before filming.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Trey O'Brien

chunks.heading: Qualification / Due Diligence
chunks.text:
### Qualification / Due Diligence

- Qualification should consider business legitimacy, ownership/founder role, online reputation, proof of operation, brand alignment, and product/show fit.
- Risk categories such as adult/suggestive content, cannabis, MLM/affiliate-only models, franchise cases, authors, personal brands, and regulated professions need a formal approved rubric.

chunks.heading: Qualification / Show Fit
chunks.text:
### Qualification / Show Fit

- Financial advisors are good candidates for the network/show.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- America’s Best Builders should be geared toward infrastructure, construction, architecture, and similar businesses; restaurant/food-truck businesses were routed toward Legacy Makers in the captured case.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Marketplace/social-platform businesses are not automatically rejected, but owned business assets and strong qualification matter.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Substantial influencer/content income can qualify as personal brand/business, but OF-led positioning was treated as a rejection case.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer and Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: adult-content policy required.

chunks.heading: Refunds / Payments / Contracts
chunks.text:
### Refunds / Payments / Contracts

- Refunds, payment pauses, custom plans, custom links, and unsigned-contract/payment situations should be legal/finance-controlled.
- The bot should not give autonomous legal or refund decisions. It can route reps to the approved escalation path once defined.
- Reps should use current finance-controlled payment instructions only; bank/payment details should not be copied into FAQ evidence.

chunks.heading: Reporting / Dashboard
chunks.text:
### Reporting / Dashboard

- Double bookings count as two calls, but daily stats call count is only Call 1s.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: reconcile with Call 2 as follow-up reporting.

- Rep dashboard key metrics at the time were RPC and RPCT; show-up rate was not tracked and RPCT sorting shows true rank.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: verify against current dashboard implementation.

chunks.heading: Reporting / Post-Close
chunks.text:
## Reporting / Post-Close

- Call 1 counts as scheduled calls; Call 2 counts as follow-up in daily stats.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Alejandra Perez and confirmed by Madeline Cary

- After close: confirm payment and signed contract, send onboarding email, book onboarding call, post in PayMe channel, and include in EOD daily stats.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Reporting / Routing
chunks.text:
### Reporting / Routing

- Once reps are live on calls, they no longer need to post in the new-hire onboarding channel.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Raul Rios

- Missed approvals can be added as a comment in the rep's daily-stats thread, with the greenlight-letter owner tagged.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- If a pass-off call ends early after the video and before completing the audition, report it in EOD as disqualified or lost.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Extra calls can be posted to the pass-off route, but the rep should run the audition if no one picks them up.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Require Current Routing Decision for Ticket Desk vs Slack
chunks.text:
### Require Current Routing Decision for Ticket Desk vs Slack

- The ticket-desk rollout and later sales-specific Slack routing comments conflict.
- Before chatbot implementation, leadership must confirm whether sales reps should use `#sales-tech-requests`, the support desk, or both by issue type and urgency.
- The bot should avoid answering "where do I post this?" from historical Slack alone.

chunks.heading: Routing / Links
chunks.text:
## Routing / Links

- Mastermind event/ticket link candidate: `[internal link]
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Mark Jefferson and Madeline Cary

- Money Mondays training link requests should be routed to fulfillment hotline as post-sale questions.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Thanksgiving/Miami filming-date availability and similar post-sale filming-date questions should be routed to fulfillment hotline.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Newer shows being cast, such as Blue Collar America at the time of the thread, may not yet have live ISTV episodes.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: show availability changes over time; verify before use.

- Love Experts was not considered brand new in the thread; it needed to be added to the offers sheet, and the show URL shared was `[internal link]
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: verify current offers sheet before use.

chunks.heading: Sales Claims / Assets
chunks.text:
### Sales Claims / Assets

- Do not discuss ROI or make leading ROI remarks; this is an FTC compliance issue.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Reps can say numbers/investment are discussed on Call 2 if approved; stick to the script when investment amount comes up on Call 1.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Clients are not provided episode-stat dashboards, except pre-promotional stats.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: reporting/claim language needs approval.

- Production/editing timeline was described as 4-6 months, typically around 5 months depending on Miami timing, with possible 2-3 month rush after filming.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary, Rich Allen, and Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: phrase as estimate, not guarantee.

- For ISTV, 5-year guaranteed placement was approved by Rudy in-thread, with exceptions if the company is sold/closed or the client does something bad.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: must match written contract before KB use.

- VIP podcast is not personally hosted by Rudy anymore; it is run by the studio team. One episode can probably include three people, ideally two.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: offer/video wording must be updated and approved.

chunks.heading: Scheduling / Cohort
chunks.text:
### Scheduling / Cohort

- Ordinary work conflicts are not a valid reason to move Call 2 beyond the cohort deadline; illness/injury needs written proof.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer

- If Call 1 has not happened and the applicant has not been greenlit, they can reschedule/reaudition.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Emergency extension requests need written proof and management review for FTC compliance.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rudy Mawer

chunks.heading: Scheduling / Cohort / Discounts
chunks.text:
### Scheduling / Cohort / Discounts

- Call 2 should be locked in before Sunday 11:59 PM EST for the cohort; a prospect who can only do Monday cannot simply be pushed.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-risk compliance item; verify current cohort rule.

- Same-day discount should not be extended to the next day just because the prospect replies after the rep is unavailable.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-risk pricing item; needs exact approved wording.

- Deadline exceptions for illness can be approved by leadership with evidence; reps should not self-approve.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary; leadership approval noted in thread

- Sunday slots can be opened, but they may be unlikely to book.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen

chunks.heading: Scheduling / Cohort Handling
chunks.text:
### Scheduling / Cohort Handling

- If a potentially qualified applicant is temporarily not in the right state of mind after a hospital stay, a later call the same week may be appropriate if still inside the cutoff.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: medical-condition edge case; compliance-approved wording needed.

- A Call 2 no-show before the weekly cutoff can still be followed up with through the cutoff.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Sunday calls can be rescheduled if the rep is unavailable, but reps are expected to block unavailable calendar time.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer

- Short-notice pre-call emails should still be sent; wording can be adjusted to "today's meeting" when the call is less than 24 hours away.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: current template source should control exact wording.

chunks.heading: Scheduling / Follow-Up
chunks.text:
### Scheduling / Follow-Up

- If an applicant is in Zoom but does not speak or turn camera on, reps can disconnect after 5 minutes and follow up by phone/SMS.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- For public/personal calendar scheduling, reps can set hours for 2nd calls and reschedules, but should not overlap or touch the master calendar.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen

- STOP alone should be verified by email or phone before canceling a scheduled audition; if no response, join the Zoom and mark no-show after the 5-minute rule. STOP plus Cancel Call points back to the onboarding opt-out/cancel SOP.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary; SOP referenced by Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: source SOP must be authoritative.

- Medical emergency can justify a casting extension, including before Call 1.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: reconcile with proof requirements in other exception threads.

- Newer/onboarding reps may be directed to dial-out only, not dummy calls, depending on Rich/management guidance.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen and Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: reconcile with onboarding docs/forms.

chunks.heading: Scheduling / Follow-Up
chunks.text:
- If someone sends STOP on a Call 1 confirmation text, do not assume they will no-show. Post their info in the opt-out channel/spreadsheet.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Ruben De Gyves and Landria Onkka

- If a Call 2 cancellation is due to family emergency and the prospect still wants to proceed, they may be eligible for next week's cohort with proof. To stop approval letters/PDFs, DM Adedokun, Sheena, or Mariela.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Family medical emergency before final Call 2 may justify pushing the call to next week if proof/evidence is provided and the prospect wants to continue. Rich said evidence should be sent to him.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rich Allen

- Medical/family emergency involving the prospect's child may justify a Call 2 exception only with medical proof/evidence for FTC audit purposes.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Casting cohort ends every Sunday at 11:59 PM EST. Call 2 cannot be moved to the following week except for catastrophic events with proof. If the applicant does not close by cohort end, the team moves on, rejection letter is sent, and the applicant can reapply after 6 months.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-risk compliance/process item; reconcile with training docs before approval.

- Sunday green-light letters can be handled by Sheena; weekend coverage noted as Adedokun Friday-Saturday and Sheena Saturday-Sunday, with Mariela as another possible support contact.
  - Sources:
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
    - [internal link]
  - Confidence note: verify current rota before approval.

chunks.heading: Scheduling / Follow-Up
chunks.text:
## Scheduling / Follow-Up

- Calendars may be left open later than 6 PM, but late calls may be unlikely to book.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: informal wording, needs approval.

- Missed/unclaimed pass-off call clients should be followed up with and may be rebooked.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Greg Easthouse and Madeline Cary

- For day-of SMS reminders, do not send before 5 AM; 6 AM may be safer.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: informal wording, needs approval.

- Call 1/audition should be done over Zoom as company policy.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Leads can only be pushed to the following week for catastrophic events such as illness, injury, death in family, or car accident; proof is required for FTC compliance. Vacation does not apply.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-risk compliance/process item; requires approval.

- If a greenlit candidate cancels after the end-of-day report, changes should be made in the stats reply thread and Adedokun should be tagged to cancel. Replacement green-light candidates should also be added in the reply thread.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Maya Ahmed; confirmed by Madeline Cary

- If someone is marked opted-out in Keap but has a scheduled call, do not cancel. Join as normal; after 5 minutes no-show, follow up by phone/text; then mark no-show if still no response.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

chunks.heading: Scheduling / Greenlight / Reporting
chunks.text:
### Scheduling / Greenlight / Reporting

- If a prospect requests cancellation by message but does not cancel their booking, the rep can cancel it from their side via the OnceHub booking link and record the reason as applicant-requested cancellation.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- For a no-show who asks to reschedule after meeting start time, creating a new booking is safer, and reps should use their public/private calendar, never the master calendar.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Before Call 1/audition, next-week reschedule may be okay; after Call 1, applicants need to commit in the same week as Call 1.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Same-day Call 2 can proceed if the greenlight owner is unavailable and the letter is not ready.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Raul Rios and Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: verify current greenlight SLA.

- Reps request ASAP greenlight letters by DMing the greenlight owner with required details.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Confidence note: current owner/required fields should be maintained outside the chatbot.

- Posting greenlit applicants in daily stats appears to be enough to report the day and start the greenlight process.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Daily-stats "follow ups booked" appears to mean new calls booked into the rep calendar, not spreadsheet follow-ups.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: asked Raul to confirm; needs final SOP confirmation.

chunks.heading: Scheduling / Greenlight Operations
chunks.text:
### Scheduling / Greenlight Operations

- Same-day greenlight requests should preferably be sent by 1 PM EST to the greenlight owner; the owner may sometimes work until around 3 PM EST. Sunday coverage was named separately.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Adedokun Adedoyin
  - Confidence note: owner rota is time-sensitive and should not be hard-coded without current verification.

- Early follow-up calls need greenlight/PDF needs flagged the night before in the daily-stats thread so they can be done first.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Celia Sarkisian and Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: greenlight workflow/SLA source needed.

- If a greenlight letter is not ready before Call 2, the rep can still discuss the likely episode rollout based on Call 1 and process payment; the client can review the letter after and work with production on changes if needed.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: high-risk sales/process item; needs owner approval.

- For late pass-off calls after EOD stats are already posted, add a comment in the thread of the EOD stat.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: peer guidance
  - Confidence note: confirm with current stats owner.

chunks.heading: Security, Privacy, and Opt-Out Topics Need Escalation-First Answers
chunks.text:
### Security, Privacy, and Opt-Out Topics Need Escalation-First Answers

- Suspicious logins, unauthorized access, wrong materials sent, applicant access to recordings, opt-out/DNC, and clients receiving messages after purchase are high-risk.
- The chatbot should immediately route these to the approved security/privacy/compliance owner and avoid extended troubleshooting.

chunks.heading: Show / Applicant Fit
chunks.text:
- For ISTV criminal-history cases, Rich said past jail time can generally be acceptable depending on the crime and judgment, but serious/violent crimes and open cases/lawsuits should not proceed until resolved. For Daymond John, any criminal history is generally no except very minor items like a speeding ticket.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen, with follow-up clarifications from Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: background-check/compliance item; requires approval before KB use.

- Prospects who disrespect/trash Rudy or the network may be DQ as poor fit.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: needs professional approved wording.

chunks.heading: Show / Applicant Fit
chunks.text:
### Show / Applicant Fit

- Mortgage brokers can qualify; independent contractors qualify only if it is their own business/entity.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- For America's Top Lawyers, reps should avoid taking those calls unless assigned/told to take them; if taken through pass-off, do not play the end of video 2 where it mentions lawyers.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: show-specific training restriction; verify current rule.

- Love Experts was newer but active enough that it needed to be added to the show list at the time.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary
  - Confidence note: date-sensitive show status.

- All shows cast multiple seasons, 15 per season.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rudy Mawer
  - Confidence note: verify current show capacity/source sheet.

- Open criminal charges would not be a fit; the team checks background rather than running a formal full background check.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: high-risk compliance item; requires owner/legal approval.

chunks.heading: Show / Applicant Fit
chunks.text:
## Show / Applicant Fit

- America's Best Doctors may have a nursing subcategory, but not a show specifically catered to nursing because the applicant must own their own business/practice.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Applicants generally must own a business. If they do not own a business, they are DQ. This applies broadly, not only healthcare.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- Authors can be a fit for the authors show if they have published a real book.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- A prospect who claims not to own a business but says they are an entrepreneur with a patent pending may be worth taking to Call 1 to clarify, because that may indicate they do have a business.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Kraig Price and Madeline Cary

- BDSM/adult-suggestive business was treated as DQ / too suggestive.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- A physician assistant may be a better fit for Live Longer than America's Best Doctors.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary

- For DJ, criminal charge likely will not pass; DJ background checks need to be very tight. ISTV may be more flexible.
  - Source: [internal link]
  - Stated by: Madeline Cary and Rich Allen
  - Confidence note: background-check/compliance item; requires approval.

chunks.heading: Slack Decision Candidates
chunks.text:
# Slack Decision Candidates

These are possible decisions or current-practice statements found in Slack. They require human approval before becoming KB articles.

chunks.heading: Source Governance
chunks.text:
### Source Governance

- Treat this channel as an announcements and source-link discovery channel. Final chatbot answers should cite approved KB articles or current source docs, not Slack announcements alone.
- When Slack links to a source doc, the source doc should be read and compared before answer drafting.
- Because the channel purpose changed over time, historical question-answer use must be separated from later no-questions announcement use.

chunks.heading: Tool Access
chunks.text:
### Tool Access

- Gmail, Zoom, and Slack are initial setup items; Keap and OnceHub appear to be set up after passing final mock or when the rep is ready to go live.
  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, January channel start template and repeated tool handoff messages.
  - Confidence note: Zoom Phone timing still needs confirmation.

- OnceHub and Keap should be accessed through Google sign-in; reps should not edit booking links themselves.
  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, repeated tool handoff template.
  - Confidence note: verify current Welcome Instructions doc.

- Zoom Phone calls and SMS should be done from the Zoom desktop or phone app, not the browser.
  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, repeated tool handoff template.

chunks.heading: Training / Meetings
chunks.text:
### Training / Meetings

- Training schedules changed repeatedly. A June schedule update was explicitly for ISTV reps only and did not apply to Daymond John reps.
  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, June 2026 schedule thread.
  - Confidence note: chatbot should not answer schedule questions from historical Slack alone.

- The 9:20-10:00 AM new-rep meeting was clarified as a meeting for newly live reps in their first two weeks, not onboarding reps.
  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, February 2026 clarification.
  - Confidence note: verify current meeting structure.

chunks.heading: Treat Sales-Tech Slack as Discovery Evidence Only
chunks.text:
### Treat Sales-Tech Slack as Discovery Evidence Only

- `#sales-tech-requests` should be used for recurring-pattern discovery, routing needs, and gap detection, not as a final policy authority.
- It is PII-heavy and contains many case-specific exceptions, outdated links, urgent one-offs, and unresolved questions.
- Approved KB answers should cite current owner-approved docs, sheets, SOPs, or leadership decisions instead of raw Slack messages.

chunks.heading: Use Checkpoints for Future Slack Refreshes
chunks.text:
### Use Checkpoints for Future Slack Refreshes

- Every completed Slack channel now has a latest-observed timestamp and a 300-second overlap rule in `source-manifest.json`.
- Future refreshes should start from the overlap timestamp, dedupe locally by Slack timestamp/permalink, and curate only messages newer than the previous checkpoint.
- Checkpoints reduce repeat collection cost; they do not approve new Slack content for chatbot use.

chunks.heading: Zoom / Screen Share Safety
chunks.text:
### Zoom / Screen Share Safety

- Reps should not share their full desktop on Zoom; they should share only the specific window/document needed and avoid exposing Slack or internal docs.
  - Source: `#new-sales-closer-onboarding`, April and May 2026 warnings.
  - Confidence note: strong candidate for an approved safety article.

Impact: Bot cannot answer refund or cancellation questions until finance/legal confirms the current matrix.

Source pointers: `slack/evidence/conflict-candidates.md` sections "Refund / Cooling-Off Window by Product", "Refund Window / Contract Language Changed", "Refundability, Contract Timing, And Consumer Law", "#2026-main... Refund / Cancellation / Cooling-Off Rules".

chunks.heading: Conflicts Log
chunks.text:
# Conflicts Log

Status: Phase 0 consolidated conflict log. Detailed raw candidates remain in `slack/evidence/conflict-candidates.md`.

This file groups the material conflicts and currency risks that block approved chatbot answers. It does not resolve them.

These items were found during hidden beta testing and must be handled before broader rollout.

chunks.heading: 2026-07-07 Admin Review Triage Gap Notes
chunks.text:
## 2026-07-07 Admin Review Triage Gap Notes

Read-only Neon review of 50 logged misses showed that the admin queue should not be treated as one generic KB backlog.

Current triage split:

- 15 wording-cleanup items.
- 11 approved-topic matching items.
- 10 Rich/owner approval gaps.
- 12 good route reviews.
- 1 runtime reliability item.
- 1 good answer review.

Disposition: dashboard admin review now computes read-only review categories/actions for misses, feedback, and recent answers. This does not approve new KB content and does not mutate Neon. The Rich/owner approval gaps still need answers from Rich or the accountable owner before they become approved KB articles.

chunks.heading: 2026-07-07 Follow-Up Matching And Scoped RAG Hardening
chunks.text:
## 2026-07-07 Follow-Up Matching And Scoped RAG Hardening

The live 18-question regression check found one approved-topic matching miss:

- "After 31 days with no contact, can another rep call the lead?"

Disposition: fixed in the policy guard and test bank.

- `runtime/policy-guard-rules.json` now maps 31-day / ownership-window / another-rep wording to the approved `twenty-percent-dial-out-sop` article when paired with lead/contact/Keap/call wording.
- `tests/kb-routing-test-bank.json` includes `OUTBOUND-003` for this exact question.
- Dashboard runtime now uses recent chat context for short or context-dependent follow-up policy matching, while keeping the latest user question authoritative.
- Approved-topic generation now allows tightly scoped supporting RAG chunks while keeping the approved article as the controlling source.

Remaining risk:

- Continue monitoring Neon/admin logs for other false abstains, especially follow-ups that omit the original product/show/topic.
- Do not re-enable broad Slack/RAG answering for unmatched policy questions.
- Speed optimization remains pending.
- Weekly checkpoint-based Slack refresh/summarization automation remains pending.

chunks.heading: 2026-07-07 Rich/Mike Meeting Gap Closure
chunks.text:
- Opt-out / DNC / STOP handling: stop normal outreach, complete DNC/Keap/opt-out steps, post to `ft-opt-outs`, and cancel pending calls after clear opt-out.
- 20 percent lead ownership: check Keap before dialing, first booking wins, 30-day ownership starts from logged communication, each new logged contact refreshes it, and after 31 days without contact another rep may speak with the lead.
- Qualification / show fit: doctors can qualify for America's Best Doctors even when hospital-employed; nurses do not qualify as doctors; PT/physio and sensitive background/reputation cases route; DJ/NLCEO background rules are stricter than main ISTV.
- Contract boundary: reps can send the current contract link, but no edits/addenda/custom clauses/legal interpretation should be promised; route exceptions to Rich/contracts/legal.
- Main ISTV Call 2 cohort reschedules: same-week reschedules are okay without Rich approval; next-week Call 2 needs Rich approval except documented medical emergency or bank-block proof. This rule is not for DJ/NLCEO.
- Failed-payment boundary: finance follows up first; if later scheduled payments fail after the initial payment, that does not remove the same-day discount.
- Scam / bad reviews objection: keep it brief, explain UK Inside Success name confusion where useful, use InsideSuccessTV context, and do not argue if the prospect believes it is a scam.
- Platform/value boundary: no vendor-cost/value comparison, no ROI/lead promises, and no paid extra Apple TV guarantee.
- Missing show dropdown: route to tech / sales ops; do not choose a wrong or placeholder show.
- America's Top Lawyers passoff: passoff/dummy-channel membership is the approval control; do not play the final part of Video 2 that mentions lawyers.
- Mastermind / red-carpet: included in all pa

chunks.heading: 2026-07-08 Custom Payment And Critical Answer Guard Closure
chunks.text:
## 2026-07-08 Custom Payment And Critical Answer Guard Closure

Rich's latest custom-payment-plan answer supplied by the user closed the remaining ambiguity around custom plans:

- Reps cannot suggest custom payment plans, custom splits, custom amounts, or custom payment links.
- Reps should use only the approved listed payment/installment plans from the current spreadsheet/source.
- Finance routing still applies for operational payment issues such as failed payments, duplicate charges, payment status, card updates, wire/ACH, invoice requests, refunds, and billing questions.
- The chatbot should not phrase custom payment plans as something finance may approve.
- Sales-tech/finance routing wording was tightened so custom payment terms, custom splits, custom amounts, and custom links are not treated as finance-approvable exceptions.

Runtime guardrail added:

- AI remains the normal answer writer for approved topics.
- High-risk approved facts now have targeted answer validation, one AI repair attempt, and a narrow approved fallback if repair/provider generation fails.
- Covered guardrails: no custom payment plans, main ISTV upgrade discount carry-forward, sales-tech route, greenlight route, main ISTV 3-month reapply minimum, and wire/ACH finance route.

Validation:

- FAQ policy guard: 92 / 92.
- FAQ answer contract/runtime: 100 / 100.
- Dashboard static Ask Sales FAQ validator: 50 / 50.
- Dashboard lint/build and `git diff --check`: passed.

Still pending:

- Signed-in allowlisted manual retest of the listed regression questions.
- Speed optimization.
- Weekly checkpoint-based Slack refresh/summarization automation.

chunks.heading: 2026-07-08 DJ/NLCEO Delayed-Payment Wording Guard
chunks.text:
## 2026-07-08 DJ/NLCEO Delayed-Payment Wording Guard

Signed-in testing showed the DJ/NLCEO cohort answer was directionally correct but one phrase was too confident: it sounded like the rep could approve a specific future first-payment date.

Disposition: minor dashboard-only wording guard implemented, pushed, production-deployed, and guard-verified.

- DJ/NLCEO cohort/payment-timing answers can say the main ISTV cohort rule does not block the client.
- They must not approve a specific future payment date or hold.
- They should route or confirm delayed first-payment / hold exceptions before promising.
- Dashboard validator, lint, typecheck, build, `git diff --check`, and a targeted no-provider runtime smoke passed.
- Dashboard commit `225ef74`; Vercel Production deployment `dpl_GbJanatQLFwsmcDE89xudLqWvnpp`.

chunks.heading: 2026-07-08 False-Abstain And Follow-Up Routing Gap Update
chunks.text:
## 2026-07-08 False-Abstain And Follow-Up Routing Gap Update

The user's hidden-beta testing found that the old policy guard was too brittle for some approved topics:

- "Are dispensaries allowed?" and a greenlight-style dispensary follow-up fell back even though the approved qualification article covers regulated cannabis businesses.
- A DJ/NLCEO deposit-timing follow-up fell back because the current message mentioned cohort/time/deposit but did not repeat "DJ show"; the old context policy matcher only handled very short follow-ups.

Disposition: dashboard runtime hardening implemented, pushed, production-deployed, and guard-verified.

- Hard policy rules remain first and unchanged in purpose.
- A high-confidence approved-article router now runs only after `default-abstain`; it can choose an existing approved article or no article, but it cannot answer freely.
- Recent context is available for context-dependent follow-up routing across the last bounded chat turns.
- Router-selected answers get an extra grounding validation against the selected approved guidance.
- Critical answer guardrails now cover regulated cannabis/dispensary qualification and DJ/NLCEO no-cohort/deposit timing.

Validation:

chunks.heading: 2026-07-08 Rich Follow-Up And Channel Routing Closure
chunks.text:
## 2026-07-08 Rich Follow-Up And Channel Routing Closure

The user's follow-up with Rich and the user's routing confirmations closed several more launch-relevant gaps without approving broad troubleshooting or finance/legal exception outcomes.

Now approved for rep-facing runtime:

- Custom payment plans, custom splits, custom amounts, and custom payment links are not allowed; reps should use only approved listed payment/installment plans from the current spreadsheet/source.
- `#sales-finance-requests` is the route for most finance/client billing questions: failed payments, duplicate charges, payment status, card updates, wire/ACH, invoice requests, refund/payment exceptions, and similar money-handling questions.
- `#sales-tech-requests` is the route for sales-tech/tooling questions: calls, Zoom, Zoom Phone, Keap, forms/dropdowns, recordings, calendars/rebooking tooling, checkout/page errors, broken payment links, and other sales-system issues.
- `#sales-questions-requests` is the route for sales-policy or approval ambiguity.
- `#greenlight-requests` is the route for greenlight letter requests, urgent sends, same-day greenlight letter requests, and current letter status/escalations.
- Reps should post in the relevant channel; the bot should not name one default individual to tag for all issues.
- Main ISTV no-show, missed Sunday 11:59 PM ET payment/signature deadline, rejected/not-fit, and similar reapply cases: tell the prospect they can reapply in the future; actual minimum is 3 months.
- Genuine proof exceptions such as a car crash or death in the family route to Rich for approval.
- Daymond John / Next Level CEO has no cohort rule and no same-day discount. Do not apply main ISTV cohort/deadline/discount rules there.

Still pending after this closure:

chunks.heading: 2026-07-09 Composer Draft Preservation Gap
chunks.text:
## 2026-07-09 Composer Draft Preservation Gap

Signed-in use found a UI-only queue gap:

- If a rep typed a newer draft while the bot was answering, a prior completion or queued auto-send could clear the composer even though the newer draft had not been submitted.

Disposition: dashboard UI-state fix implemented, pushed, production-deployed, and verified.

- The composer now clears only when its current text matches the exact question being sent or queued.
- Different current draft text is preserved through answer completion and queued auto-sends.
- Dashboard Ask Sales FAQ validator: 69 / 69.
- Dashboard code commit: `2feef7d` (`Preserve Ask Sales FAQ composer drafts`).
- Vercel Production deployment reached Ready: `dpl_AJ1MdM3bg6o58cXsQHogoxhANx8D`.
- Anonymous page/API guards still return sign-in / `not_signed_in`.

Remaining risk:

- Signed-in manual browser confirmation is still needed after deployment: type during an in-flight answer, let the answer finish, and confirm the draft remains.
