chunks.heading: 2026-07-09 Final Presentation Polish Gap
chunks.text:
## 2026-07-09 Final Presentation Polish Gap

Signed-in retest plus Neon log review after the source-routing pass found no policy-critical wrong answer in the latest 28-question set. The remaining items were small polish gaps:

- duplicate adjacent structured section titles such as repeated `What you can do`;
- explicit short DJ/NLCEO fallback wording was safe but longer than ideal;
- Vercel could show critical-repair warning noise even when a valid approved fallback was returned to the rep.

Disposition: dashboard runtime polish implemented, pushed, locally verified, and production-deployed.

- Adjacent duplicate display sections now merge generically after existing section normalization.
- The DJ/NLCEO short critical fallback is shorter but still includes the required no-cohort, no-same-day-discount, main-ISTV-not-applicable, listed-payment-only, and no-promise boundary.
- Critical-repair warnings now wait until fallback validation fails, so valid final fallback answers do not create misleading warning logs.
- Dashboard Ask Sales FAQ validator: 68 / 68.
- Dashboard `npm run lint`: passed.
- Dashboard `npm run build`: passed.
- Dashboard touched-file `git diff --check`: passed.
- Dashboard code commit `a2ec01a`.
- Vercel Production deployment `dpl_GarRvRj6cWQ4HJoYXLVx8CHkroBW` reached Ready.

Remaining risk:

- User signed-in spot check is still needed, but this is now a launch-polish verification item rather than an open source/content gap.

chunks.heading: 2026-07-09 Natural Conversation Follow-up Gap
chunks.text:
## 2026-07-09 Natural Conversation Follow-up Gap

Signed-in testing showed the bot still had a naturalness gap:

- Short confirmations could trigger a long repeated policy answer.
- Thank-you/social turns could trigger the generic safe fallback.

Disposition: narrow dashboard runtime fix implemented, pushed, production-deployed, and guard-verified.

- A DeepSeek conversation planner now runs only after the hard policy guard reaches `default-abstain`.
- The planner can return only `conversation_reply`, `approved_article`, or `unsupported`.
- `conversation_reply` is limited to acknowledgments, short confirmations, and shorten/rephrase requests that can be answered from recent assistant context.
- The planner cannot add policy, prices, discounts, owners, links, exceptions, or process steps.
- Successful conversation replies do not create admin miss rows.
- No hard-coded social replies, caching, model downgrade, or broad free-answer path was added.
- Dashboard commit `96824fa`; FAQ docs commit `b3e823c`; Vercel Production deployment `dpl_JDh5X1zwoSemP69zaWLffQbwt7h6`.
- Anonymous page/API guards passed and Vercel `/api/ask-sales-faq` runtime errors showed none after deployment checks.

Remaining risk:

- Signed-in production retesting is still needed after deployment.
- Continue watching logs for false positives where a new policy question is incorrectly treated as a conversation reply.
- Do not solve future naturalness issues by adding broad deterministic synonym lists or fallback-to-any-approved-answer behavior.

chunks.heading: 2026-07-09 Naturalness / Policy-Authority Boundary Gap
chunks.text:
## 2026-07-09 Naturalness / Policy-Authority Boundary Gap

Signed-in follow-up testing after the short-follow-up pass showed a broader naturalness and authority-boundary issue:

- Conversation replies could still look like forced structured cards instead of normal chat.
- Planner summaries such as "Brief friendly reply..." could appear in the UI.
- A fresh payment/action question could be accepted as `conversation_reply`, which is unsafe because it bypasses approved article authority.
- Short-answer requests could still get long DJ/NLCEO critical fallback wording.

Disposition: dashboard runtime fix implemented, pushed, production-deployed, locally verified, and production guard/log verified.

chunks.heading: 2026-07-09 Naturalness / Policy-Authority Boundary Gap
chunks.text:
- The DeepSeek planner remains in the loop for natural follow-up replies.
- Runtime now rejects `conversation_reply` for new sales-policy/action questions involving offers, promises, deposits, payments, discounts, holds, exceptions, qualification, greenlights, contracts, links, cohorts, or deadlines.
- Conversation replies now render as plain chat text instead of policy cards.
- Conversation structured payloads reload correctly with `sourceMode: "conversation"`.
- Approved-answer prompt wording now asks for the shortest useful live-call answer first.
- DJ/NLCEO critical fallback can return a concise safe answer when the rep explicitly asks for a short reply.
- Dashboard static Ask Sales FAQ validator: 65 / 65.
- Dashboard `npm run lint`: passed.
- Dashboard `npm run build`: passed.
- Dashboard `npx tsc --noEmit`: passed after `next build` generated the required Next type artifacts.
- Dashboard code commit `327c483`.
- Vercel Production deployment `dpl_2mwVzPeVPoTev6eNYHmT7mxzg2bS` reached Ready.
- Production alias: `[internal link]
- Anonymous page/API guards passed.
- Vercel build and runtime log checks showed no build errors, runtime errors, warnings, or fatal logs after guard probes.

Remaining risk:

- Signed-in production retest is still needed now that deployment is verified.
- Watch logs for both sides of the boundary: new policy/action questions must not become casual replies, and true conversational turns should not become generic safe fallbacks.
- Do not solve remaining naturalness issues with hard-coded social scripts, broad synonym lists, caching, model downgrade, or fallback-to-any-approved-answer behavior.

chunks.heading: 2026-07-09 Production Rollout Guardrails
chunks.text:
## 2026-07-09 Production Rollout Guardrails

Disposition: implemented in the dashboard, pushed, production-deployed, and guard-verified.

Closed for launch-hardening:

- Duplicate-send protection through browser `clientRequestId` and `ask_sales_faq_request_guards`.
- Safe replay for completed/rate-limited protected responses.
- Friendly in-progress duplicate message.
- Per-rep default accepted-request protection: 50 requests per 30 minutes.
- Global default accepted-request protection: 300 requests per 60 seconds.
- Non-blocking successful answer history logging through Next `after()`.
- Friendly user-facing temporary failure/rate-limit messages.

Still pending:

- Review live `ask_sales_faq_request_guards` rows after real signed-in use.
- Build an admin usage tracking page for per-rep chatbot usage.
- Keep the usage page separate from the existing admin logs/review page.
- Continue monitoring answer quality and latency after wider signed-in rollout.

chunks.heading: 2026-07-09 Short Follow-up Ordering And Specialist-Handoff Gap
chunks.text:
## 2026-07-09 Short Follow-up Ordering And Specialist-Handoff Gap

Signed-in retesting of the natural conversation fix found the remaining failure mode:

- "so basically i should not promise him anything right?" still went through contextual DJ/NLCEO policy routing before the planner could answer naturally.
- The answer included unapproved specialist handoff wording. That is not allowed.
- "Can you make that shorter?" repeated a long policy-style answer instead of shortening the previous substantive answer.

Disposition: dashboard runtime fix implemented, pushed, production-deployed, and guard-verified.

chunks.heading: 2026-07-09 Short Follow-up Ordering And Specialist-Handoff Gap
chunks.text:
- Direct policy rules still run first.
- Short social, confirmation, and rewrite follow-ups now reach the DeepSeek conversation planner before contextual policy routing.
- The planner can still only return `conversation_reply`, `approved_article`, or `unsupported`.
- If the planner does not resolve the turn, the existing context-aware policy route still runs.
- Planner and answer prompts now block "connect you with a specialist" / "someone will reach out" language unless an approved source explicitly allows that handoff.
- The rep-facing wording repair treats the same specialist-handoff phrases as disallowed output.
- Dashboard static Ask Sales FAQ validator: 62 / 62.

Remaining risk:

- Signed-in production retest is still needed after deployment.

- Keep the fix narrow; do not replace it with broad deterministic synonym lists, caching, or fallback-to-any-approved-answer behavior.

chunks.text:
## 2026-07-09 Short-Answer And Option-List Formatting Gap

Signed-in testing found one follow-on presentation gap:

- The exact duplicate paragraph/card issue was fixed, but explicit one-line questions could still show a short summary plus a long structured `Answer` card.
- Dense payment plans could be packed into one paragraph instead of bullets, making the response harder to scan during a live call.

Disposition: dashboard runtime presentation fix implemented, pushed, locally verified, and production-deployed.

- Explicit short-answer requests now suppress extra structured sections in the visible payload.
- Dense payment/package option paragraphs can be reshaped into bullet items without adding facts or changing policy routing.
- Dashboard Ask Sales FAQ validator: 66 / 66.

Remaining risk:

chunks.heading: 2026-07-09 Source Routing And Presentation Contract Retest Gaps
chunks.text:
## 2026-07-09 Source Routing And Presentation Contract Retest Gaps

Disposition after the broader 28-question review:

- Greenlight-letter route: no longer treated as an unknown channel. Runtime routing is hardened so greenlight letter requests, urgent sends, and status/escalations go to `#greenlight-requests`, not `#sales-finance-requests`.
- Commission tier / leaderboard / Bill.com / payout: still a known gap/deferred area for runtime answers. The bot should not infer a rep's live tier or payout from finance/payment guidance; it now fails closed and tells the rep to check the current sales/commission owner.
- Current-show and payment-plan formatting: presentation issue, not a KB-approval issue. The runtime now reshapes existing generated show/payment list text into scannable list items where possible.
- Section-title naturalness: presentation issue, not a source-content issue. `What you can say` should be used for literal suggested wording; action instructions should use `What you can do`.

Remaining risk:

- User still needs to rerun the same 28 signed-in questions and then have the logs reviewed for route source, answer quality, formatting, source-card support, naturalness, and latency.

chunks.heading: Answer Voice
chunks.text:
### Answer Voice

Some answers still speak in third person. Rep-facing answers should use direct second-person wording:

- "You can say..."
- "Do not promise..."
- "Route this..."
- "If the client asks..."

Disposition: dashboard prompt and sanitizer were updated on 2026-07-03 to push direct second-person wording. Continue monitoring live beta answers.

- In that narrow case, price can be mentioned to disqualify them.

chunks.heading: High-Risk Policy Gaps
chunks.text:
## High-Risk Policy Gaps

- Refund/cancellation/deposit/payment pause/failed payment/duplicate charge outcomes. Product-level refund rules and finance-channel routing are approved, but exception outcomes still require review.
- Payment link/method/card-update/wire/ACH/invoice matrix. Custom payment plans/splits/amounts are now closed as not allowed; reps should use only approved listed plans. Exact payment-method instructions, bank/wire details, and finance exception outcomes still require finance approval.
- Contract edit/addendum/legal review path. Current no-edit/no-addendum route boundary is approved, but detailed legal exception SOP remains pending.
- Public proof, views, demographics, rankings, testimonials, ROI, lead-generation, and business-outcome wording. Distribution and celebrity allow-list rules are clearer after the Magic Mike ISTV context pack; public share links and exact rep-safe wording remain unapproved.
- Greenlight/cohort/reapply/no-show/same-day discount matrix. Main ISTV 3-month reapply minimum, main ISTV Call 2 cohort rule, greenlight-letter route, and DJ/NLCEO no-cohort/no-discount are now approved; exact caps/send windows/status mechanics remain pending.
- Qualification/background/reputation/adult/regulated/political edge-case rubric.
- Content rights and usage rights by package/product.
- Opt-out/DNC/privacy/security escalation process.

chunks.heading: Internal KB Status Language
chunks.text:
### Internal KB Status Language

Before wider launch, rep-facing answers must not say:

- "not approved in the knowledge base"
- "pending approval"
- "route-only"
- "approved article"
- "RAG"
- "manifest"
- "source coverage"
- article filenames or internal article IDs

Rep-facing missing-answer fallback should be normal and useful:

- "I do not have a confirmed answer for that yet. Route this to the current sales owner or the right help channel before replying to the prospect."

Disposition: dashboard prompt, sanitizer, fallback copy, and validator coverage were updated on 2026-07-03. Dashboard runtime was hardened again on 2026-07-07 with full structured-output hidden-term detection, a model-based rep-facing repair pass, and stricter source-card labels. Continue monitoring live beta answers for new wording leaks.

chunks.text:
# Known Gaps

These are not bugs in the future bot. They are topics where the source layer is not ready for approved answers yet.

chunks.heading: Lite Upgrade Discount Carry-Forward
chunks.text:
### Lite Upgrade Discount Carry-Forward

User confirmed the `$2,000` same-day discount carries forward when a main ISTV Lite buyer upgrades to Standard or VIP before filming.

Working approved rule to encode:

- Upgrade before filming only.
- Discount carries forward for main ISTV upgrades.
- Discounted Standard total: `$18,000`.
- Discounted VIP/Premium total: `$28,000`.
- If the client bought discounted Lite at `$10,000`, the difference is `$8,000` to Standard or `$18,000` to VIP/Premium.
- Use proper upgraded contract and payment-difference link / sales-tech or finance route.

Disposition: resolved in the approved KB on 2026-07-03.

### Source-Card Mismatch

At least one same-day discount answer logged an unrelated matched article (`call-recording-storage-and-access`). This is a source-card trust issue, not just a UI issue.

Need source validation so the selected source card matches the evidence actually used in the answer.

chunks.heading: Speed Optimization
chunks.text:
### Speed Optimization

Speed improvement remains pending but is not the immediate correctness fix. Do not reduce answer quality to make the bot faster. Candidate future optimizations include compact article summaries, smaller evidence packets, caching stable facts, streaming UI, and faster fallback handling.

chunks.heading: Time-Sensitive Ownership Answers
chunks.text:
### Time-Sensitive Ownership Answers

Greenlight/process owner answers can become stale. Unless a person is confirmed as current owner, prefer "current sales/ops owner" or the approved help channel over naming a permanent person.

chunks.heading: 2026-07-03 Update
chunks.text:
## 2026-07-03 Update

Call 1 vs Call 2 pricing boundary is no longer in the Rich-needed bucket. Rich's 2026-07-03 Slack confirmation was converted into the approved `knowledge-base/call-process/call-1-flow.md` article:

- Default: do not discuss pricing, packages, payment plans, discounts, deposits, or license PDFs on Call 1.
- Narrow exception: if the rep is sure the prospect has no business and is not financially qualified, price may be mentioned only to disqualify them.
- Do not use the exception to pitch, close, negotiate, or pre-sell on Call 1.

11. Platform placement wording
   - ISN app wording, physical-device clarification, VIP Tier-1 submission wording, and no-guarantee platform language can be kept.

   - ISTV platform hosting is 5 years.
   - Client license rights to their own episode/content are lifetime.

   - Reps must not promise ROI, revenue, leads, fundraising, PR outcomes, platform placement, or guaranteed business results.

## Ready To Convert After Approval Metadata

These items have Syed reviewer remarks and no correction that blocks the recommended answer.

1. Current sales rule update channels
   - Use `#2026-main-all-sales-reps-no-questions` for announcements/source updates and `#sales-questions-requests` for Q&A/clarifications.
   - If they disagree, reps should not guess from memory; route to sales leadership/compliance for the current rule.

2. Current active show list source
   - Use project operations as the maintained source for current/new shows.
   - The Luke Gent show list remains useful current evidence, but the live maintained place is project operations.

3. Main ISTV package/pricing matrix
   - Lite, Standard, and VIP/Premium pricing and deliverables from the current context-pack answer were reviewed by Syed without correction.

4. Next Level CEO / Daymond John pricing
   - Lite $10,000; Standard $15,000; Premium VIP $20,000; CEO Day upgrade $5,000.

5. Same-day discount rule
   - Same-day discount applies only to the main ISTV program from Call 2 when the client pays the initial deposit on the same calendar day as that Call 2.
   - If Call 2 ends before payment but the client pays later that same calendar day, the discount can still be honored.
   - It does not apply to Next Level CEO.

6. Refund rule by product
   - Next Level CEO / Daymond John shows have a current 3-day refund window.
   - Other shows, including ISTV/main-program shows, have no refund offer and no refunds.

9. No custom payment plans, amounts, or payment links
   - Reps should use only listed payment plans and official/current links.
   - Custom amounts/plans/links and wire/ACH/invoice exceptions should be routed.

1. Convert the `approved_article_candidate` rows from Pack 1 into clean one-page draft articles.
2. Add approval provenance fields only where Syed approval is explicit and traceable.
3. Keep all `pending_rich_confirmation` rows out of runtime answers until Rich or the accountable owner confirms.

5. Use `evidence_only` rows to build the test bank and future miss categories, not runtime answers.

chunks.heading: Current Bottom Line
chunks.text:
## Current Bottom Line

The data is not lost. It is split intentionally:

- Approved-candidate material is ready for article conversion after approval metadata is handled.
- Rich/owner-confirmation material is preserved and blocked from runtime answers.
- Slack/transcript/onboarding evidence remains available for drafting, test cases, and future coverage.
- Stale or incomplete media/source material is explicitly marked so it cannot accidentally become bot truth.

chunks.heading: Source To KB Coverage Matrix
chunks.text:
# Source To KB Coverage Matrix

Status: Phase 1 control ledger, created 2026-06-30.

Purpose: make sure the collected Slack, Zoom/transcript, onboarding, Google Docs, sheets, context-pack, and review artifacts are accounted for before approved KB article conversion starts.

Important rule: this file does not approve content. It maps source clusters to a current disposition so we know whether each area is ready for article drafting, needs Rich/owner confirmation, should be route-only, should stay evidence-only, or should be deferred.

chunks.heading: Status Key
chunks.text:
## Status Key

| Status | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| `approved_article_candidate` | Sources plus Syed review are strong enough to prepare article wording, but approval metadata still must be added before runtime use. |
| `pending_rich_confirmation` | Working answer exists, but the topic needs Rich or another accountable owner before the bot can answer directly. |
| `route_only_pending` | Keep the bot from answering substance for now; route the rep to the right person/channel until an owner approves wording. |
| `known_gap_or_deferred` | Source layer is incomplete, stale, too time-sensitive, or intentionally outside V1. |
| `evidence_only` | Useful for frequency, wording, test cases, or future articles, but not an answer source. |
| `ignore_or_stale` | Do not use for approved articles unless a current owner revives it. |

source_policy.approved_article: Highest authority. May answer directly when relevant.
source_policy.curated_kb_article: Useful evidence. Answer with caution or route when status is draft/in_conflict.
source_policy.curated_slack_evidence: Discovery evidence. Useful for coverage and wording, but weaker than approved/current articles.
source_policy.governance_log: Internal governance/crosswalk evidence. Useful for conflicts, gaps, and source accounting.
source_policy.training_transcript: Training context. Useful for background and scripts, weaker than approved/current rules.
